Only the pinned calibration row (rate 1/2) gives s = 0 exactly at A=507 (reproducing the proved tangent pin — rate 1/2's knife-edge nature confirmed at calibration). No verdict depends on the B_tan ambiguity or collision direction. FLAG: dihedral-stratum mass excluded from the formula — if E25 finds it real AND it populates odd-j candidates (odd supports = pairs + one of {1,-1}, so not parity-excluded), the budget shrinks; re-run with the dihedral column then. | E30 SHARPENS THE FLAG: the clean-rate candidates sit at ODD j, and dihedral-type words DO exist there in the anti-reciprocal form — the dihedral budget column, when E25 lands, must use the corrected -+a^{-j+2m} clause, not the even-j form. | MANDATORY FOLLOW-UP (QA.21): re-run the budget with the DIHEDRAL COLUMN — E25 says the mass is real, E30 gives the odd-j anti-reciprocal clause for the candidates. The clean-rate s = 2^122+ could shrink; compute B_dih at each candidate exactly. | QA.21 NEGATIVE (76/76 green, GAP QA21-G1): the crude dihedral column exceeds B* at EVERY clean-rate candidate (+292/+158/+52 bits at Row C; ~1e11-bit scale at prize-max); the E26 window model gives 0 at odd j but provably omits the fixed-point branch (E30). NO in-repo lemma bounds per-pair dihedral simultaneity. The s >= 2^122 verdict is now CONDITIONAL on dihedral_staircase; the audit's status reverts to TARGET pending that lemma. Honest arc: the requirement computation giveth (the poly allowance) and taketh away (the dihedral column). | CLOSED by the poly-forcing compiler (52/52, exact integers): 16 n^3 <= s_lo(A) at all six candidates (prize rows tight at 29 n^3); QA21-G1 DISSOLVES — the dihedral/extension columns fold into R_post, so no separate B_dih subtraction exists; the staircase req edge removed accordingly. | QA.22 REQUIRED (X-4): re-run the candidate budget with the staircase column — staircase supports inflate SUPPORT counts not slope counts, but the census/dossier B_quot documentation and the list-side budgets need the exact staircase binomials per candidate (~2^105-115 at prize rows vs B* ~ 2^128: expected to fit with 13-23 bits of room; verify exactly). | QA.22 GATE PASSED (27/27, exact big integers): the four-column split fits under B* at ALL SIX candidates — Row C staircase margins 22.2/55.9/39.0 bits; prize rows have 27.0/60.6+ bits of staircase room after the 16n^3 + B_tan reserve (the headline 0.9-bit 'total margin' at prize rows is the aggregate-vs-B* line under QA.22's conservative convention — read the note's conventions before quoting). The transported quotient-row staircase table emitted for TR.

nodes.title: THE RIGIDITY KERNEL: the one statement the four open faces consume
nodes.statement: RK (schema): at every FM-suppressed operating point of every row and every quotient-lattice scale (multiplicative + dihedral), every agreement object is paid-taxonomy-member (tangent / multiplicative pullback / dihedral pullback / extension) or belongs to a primitive remainder <= n^{B_RK} x FM(instance), worst-case per word/pair, jointly across characters. The four consumers: TR = RK at quotient rows; the F residue = RK for evaluation flats; the spread classification = RK for rank-loss configurations; the clean-rate exclusion = RK per-pair at the base row. Proved instances: deep-mca (radius <= 1/3-distance), pincer (1/2 via CA), flats dim <= 2 + fixed-d + MDS, the moment ledger (average case, all radii), #152 (t=2 local), #212 (cross-character reduction). The frontier = excursions along five axes (radius, dimension, quantifier, deficiency, jointness); the worst-case axis is shared by all faces and is where the exceptional-object classification lives. See experimental/notes/roadmaps/rigidity_kernel.md.

nodes.title: REQUIRED LEMMA (QA21-G1): the per-pair dihedral-aligned support cap
nodes.statement: For a fixed pair (u,v), bound the number of simultaneously-aligned inversion-closed supports: the dihedral analogue of the tangent staircase. QA.21 shows the crude bound (all N_dih = 2 C((n-2)/2, (j-1)/2) supports) blows the clean-rate budget by +52..+292 bits (Row C) — the allowance survives only if per-pair dihedral simultaneity is suppressed by those factors. STRUCTURAL HANDLES: (a) every odd-j inversion-closed support contains one of exactly TWO points ({+1, -1}) — all dihedral-aligned supports pass through one of two links, so the top-core/link machinery bites maximally; (b) palindromic agreement rigidity (the aligned word's symmetric part is constrained); (c) E25's toy data shows small per-pair counts empirically (100/64 charged events across the whole corpus). Expected shape: linear-in-n like the tangent staircase.
nodes.attack_surface: start from the fixed-point observation: condition on the support containing +1; the aligned family through one point at one slope is a moving-root-type family — the tangent staircase argument should transfer nearly verbatim; E25's charging machinery is the toy calibration
nodes.falsifier: a toy pair aligning super-linear-in-n dihedral supports (searchable with E25's machinery)
nodes.notes: GPT PRO X-3 + Codex integration (verifier 36/36): PROVED in the deep regime 3j <= n-k — at most n+2 exact supports across ALL slopes, dihedrality not even needed (two supports pin (U,V); third codewords forced to the pencil; error-word zero sets change at <= n slopes). HONESTLY SCOPED: not applicable at the clean-rate candidates (j ~ 957 of 1024). The corridor obligation is REFRAMED by the poly-forcing compiler: the dihedral column folds into R_post <= 16 n^3 — no separate budget subtraction; this node's corridor content merges into the face-4 rungs. | POST-COMPILER ROLE: demoted req -> ev. Dihedral supports count inside R_post like any others, so the rungs' analysis covers them; this lemma is now a sharpening (and its deep-regime instance a proved template), not a gate. | FLIP on replay (Codex rednode wave 2): TARGET -> PROVED; packet in nodes/dihedral_staircase/.

nodes.title: FACE-4 LEG 1 (from E27): the pencil-cascade forcing lemma
nodes.statement: If a pair (u,v) has two aligned supports (distinct slopes) whose agreement sets share a common core of size >= k+t-1 = A-1, then the slope pencil is FORCED on the core (interpolation: core >= k determines both explaining codewords, the 2x2 slope system inverts), and the alignment CASCADES: every off-core point upgrades one further slope, so the pair's multiplicity is ~n-core and the pair is a TANGENT-PENCIL pair — paid. E27 verified every observed cascade algebraically at RS[F_97, mu_16, k=8] (the multiplicity-2 stratum has a -8.8 sigma HOLE at cores >= A-1: big-core mult-2 events are impossible, exactly as the lemma predicts). The argument is interpolation + the two-slope trick — scale-free.
nodes.attack_surface: specialize two_slope_intersection to cores >= k+t-1; the cascade step is one line (pencil evaluation off-core); write-up is a small packet
nodes.falsifier: a toy multiplicity-2 pair with core >= A-1 and no pencil (E27's machinery searches this directly; zero found in 2000 constructed + 10^5 organic)
nodes.notes: PROVED (W1, PR #10, 70/70 replayed): the forcing lemma at core >= k+t-1 with the cascade, scale-free, E27-calibrated. Face 4 rung 1 closed.

nodes.title: FACE-4 LEG 2 (from E27): the small-core spread-family count
nodes.statement: For every pair (u,v): the number of aligned aperiodic supports whose agreement sets pairwise share cores < k+t-1 (the post-cascade remainder — a SPREAD family in the face-3 sense) is <= poly(n). E27's toy data: this remainder sits exactly in the FM band (unstructured mult->=2 events at FM-normalized rates, all classification tests at chance) — clause (ii) of the rigidity kernel holds empirically for the remainder. This is face 4's residual content after the cascade lemma — and it is a SPREAD-FAMILY count: face 3's machinery (design bounds, syzygy circuits, the exception classes) is the natural instrument. The convergence: face 4 decomposes into a provable forcing lemma + a face-3-type bound + the dihedral staircase.

Pairwise additivity at distinct slopes is rank-level PROVED (qx13 2b); only the m >= 3 stagnation dichotomy is genuinely open. | WIRING FIX (seam audit 2026-07-04): u1_pullback_dichotomy promoted ev -> req. This puts the terminal cluster (certificates, midlarge, B2b) formally on the prize critical path, as the closure assembly requires. | FLIP on replay (Codex critical-walk batch): assembly/conditional packet in nodes/xr_smallcore_spread_count/conditional.md.

nodes.title: RUNG 2a: same-slope families ARE the list challenge's object
nodes.statement: Within one slope z, distinct aligned supports = distinct codewords at distance exactly j from u+zv, and their supports are AUTOMATICALLY far-spread (two distinct codewords agree < k, so exact agreement sets of the same word overlap r < k — qx13 Theorem 1's forced-equality case, contrapositive). So the same-slope leg of xr_smallcore_spread_count is EXACTLY a worst-word list count at radius j — the SECOND grand challenge's kernel object. THE TWO GRAND CHALLENGES MEET HERE: any per-word poly list bound at the corridor radius (the list lane's own safe-side content, with its planted-sunflower machinery, petal ledgers, and clean margins at the clean rates) plugs in directly, summed over the <= q slopes with the FM/budget arithmetic controlling the slope sum.

nodes.notes: PROVED (X-3 wave, verifier 39/39): same_slope_branch(u,v;z,A) = ExactList_C(w_z, A) exactly, with automatic far-spread (RS uniqueness + exactness). The list-lane obligation itself lives in the list nodes, as designed. | X-4 CORRECTION: the consumed object is the SPLIT form (staircase terms charged + primitive bound), not the unqualified ExactList. Slope/support distinction limits MCA-side impact: staircase supports share slopes, so R_post's slope count survives; the spread-count support analysis charges staircase supports to the (enlarged, tail-form) quotient strip.

nodes.title: RUNG 2b: the partial-forcing band (cores k+1..A-2) — a graded tangent ledger
nodes.statement: Distinct-slope aligned supports with core r in [k+1, A-2]: subtracting the two alignment identities gives (z1-z2) v = c1 - c2 on the core, so v agrees with a codeword on r >= k+1 points — the pair acquires depth-(r-k) tangent structure (one line, proved two-slope machinery). The work: design the GRADED tangent ledger charging depth-d partially-forced pairs (the s-ladder's worst-case face; #152's t=2 exchange packet is the proved base case), so that every band pair is either charged or upgraded toward the cascade threshold.
nodes.attack_surface: iterate pairwise forcing up the depth grading; the tangent staircase's shape at each depth; E27's machinery calibrates band occupancy at toys
nodes.falsifier: a toy band family resisting both charge and upgrade (E27's pencil machinery extended to partial cores)
nodes.notes: THE FORCING MAP IS PROVED (X-3 wave): every distinct-slope pair with core r = k+d, 1 <= d <= t-2, forces u = U, v = V on the core — a genuine tangent-depth-d cell; the COMPLEMENTARITY IDENTITY d + s = t (tangent depth + qx13 fresh codim = t exactly). Remaining: the ledger design (charging). Boundary r = k proved to belong with the rank/spread core. | W2 (PR #12, 26,385 checks replayed): the graded ledger DESIGN packet — depth-cell charging table built on the proved forcing map, honest non-promotion (the final ledger bound remains open; the design is the scaffold for it). | A2 (#26, 10/10 replayed): CONDITIONAL-ROUTED, routing table COMPLETE (no extra pairwise/heavy-triangle object); the two occupancy accountings are the only remaining content (nodes added, one shared with A1). | FLIP on replay (Codex rednode wave 2): TARGET -> CONDITIONAL; packet in nodes/xr_partial_tangent_band/.

nodes.title: THE IRREDUCIBLE CORE (rung 2c): the fresh-codimension-or-structure dichotomy
nodes.statement: Far-spread regime (distinct slopes, cores r < k — no pairwise forcing exists): prove that every additional aligned support either (a) raises the joint rank of the pair's stacked alignment conditions by >= 1 (then the 2n dimension cap gives per-pair count <= 2n + charged, POLY outright), or (b) certifies an algebraic identity (rank stagnation = an eliminant vanishing identically) that places the family in a paid stratum. Proved inputs: distinct-slope conditions are exactly-additive PAIRWISE (qx13 (2b): the [[1,z],[1,z']] change of variables is rank-level, not just measure-level); the m >= 3 degeneracy question is the eliminant dichotomy at family level (M5's u5 shape). THIS is where the rigidity kernel's axis-Q content genuinely lives for face 4 — everything else in the face is proved machinery, a crossover, or graded bookkeeping. Note: dimension counting ALONE cannot close it (a point lies in adversarially many t-codim subspaces); the algebraic dichotomy is the content.

Rank stagnation at a new support forces a SYZYGY: dual codewords lambda_i in Lambda_{T_i} with sum (z_i - z_m) phi_i = 0 (the twist is invertible since slopes are distinct). Support cancellation + the MDS dual weight then force: for some member i0, supp(lambda_i0) lies inside the union of its pairwise intersections, hence sum over partners |T_i0 cap T_i'| >= k+1. QUANTITATIVE CONSEQUENCE: in the far-spread regime (each overlap < k), every stagnation event needs >= 2 partners; the minimal configuration is a TRIPLE; and stagnation events are rationed by the family's total overlap mass.

nodes.title: FACE-2 KEYSTONE (PROVABLE): the quotient sector is complete by group theory
nodes.statement: Quotient-type escape mechanisms arise from Mobius symmetries of the evaluation domain; the PGL_2 stabilizer of mu_n (and of 2-power coset domains) contains the dihedral group Dih_n (rotations x -> zeta x, inversions x -> zeta/x) and generically equals it. Every subgroup of a dihedral group is cyclic or dihedral (theorem): cyclic => multiplicative pullbacks g(X^M); dihedral => Chebyshev quotients X^e g(X^M + zeta' X^-M) (twisted inversions are conjugate relabelings). THEREFORE the symmetry-quotient taxonomy {multiplicative, dihedral} is COMPLETE — no sixth quotient mechanism exists. Explains E25/E27/E30's clean fifth-shape watches structurally. Residual check (small lemma): no exotic stabilizer at special (n, q) — the stabilizer of an n-point projective set with n >= 3 is finite and contains Dih_n; rule out proper overgroups for prize domains. AMENDED (Codex P2 + verified negative controls + the Mersenne analysis): the raw all-(n,q) claim is FALSE. TAME rows: stabilizer = Dih_n PROVED (P2's tame case). WILD rows — the stabilizer explodes to PGL_2(F_{n-1}) (3-transitive, order ~n^3) exactly when the domain is projectively the subfield line P^1(F_{n-1}): requires n-1 a prime power AND F_{n-1} in F_q. For 2-power n, n-1 prime power <=> Mersenne structure: n in {4, 8, 32, 128, 2^13, 2^17, 2^19, 2^31} (exhaustive to 2^41; both negative controls verified independently: F_9/mu_4 -> 24 = S_4 by my brute force, F_49/mu_8 -> 336 = |PGL_2(F_7)|). ADMISSIBLE WILD ROWS EXIST: q = (2^m - 1)^{2s} < 2^256 with n | q - 1 automatic (q'^2 - 1 = (q'-1) 2^m for Mersenne q') at n = 2^13, 2^17, 2^19, 2^31. Row C (1023 = 3*11*31) and prize-max (2^41 - 1 composite) are TAME. At wild rows the completeness conclusion enlarges from {cyclic, dihedral} to the DICKSON subgroup lattice of PGL_2(F_{n-1}) — still finite and classical. Named condition: p2_no_wild_subfield_circle_domain (per-row tameness check, pure arithmetic).
nodes.attack_surface: the subgroup theorem is classical; the stabilizer computation is a short PGL_2 argument; the exotic-case check is finite casework (n = 2-power, n | q-1)

The 'explains the clean fifth-shape watches' note is retracted: those censuses were weight-<=4-blind.

nodes.title: FACE-2 CORE (evidence-informed): the weight-2 inverse theorem
nodes.statement: RESTATED ALONG E35's OBSERVED STRUCTURE (1452 flats, 28/28): if a flat (post common-root strip) carries >= 4 DISJOINT minimal weight-2 dual supports (the MATCHING count, not raw pairs — near-pencils reach raw 6 without symmetry but matching 2), then it is contained in a projective-multiplicative stratum X^e g(X^M) or a dihedral stratum. Empirical basis: the distribution has an EMPTY BAND — nonsymmetric flats have matching count 0-3, symmetric 7-8, nothing at 4-6 (threshold 4, margin 3); collapse under perturbation is all-or-nothing (7-8 -> <=2 in one cell), so the inverse theorem is sharp with no soft regime. PROOF PLAN (two steps): (i) matching composition — >= 4 disjoint two-point ratio constraints compose into a global symmetry candidate; (ii) the candidate lands in the FINITE target (f_dih_subgroup_completeness: cyclic or dihedral, nothing else). STEP (i) PROVED AT QUADRATIC THRESHOLD (Theorem 1, verifier 7/7: m > j + (j-1)^2 disjoint weight-2 supports force GLOBAL PULLBACK: Phi = nu compose psi, every psi-fiber pair a flat-wide ratio edge; engine = value rigidity + P1xP1 Bezout + Luroth; linear threshold W(d) = d+2 open with Lemma D as seed). STEP (ii) FALSIFIED AS STATED and CORRECTED: the conclusion space is NOT Dih_n — witness span{1, q, q^2}, q = X^2+3X over F_17: post-strip, w2m = 7, no Dih stratum, psi-fibers of the affine involution x -> -3-x. The TRUE conclusion space is the LUROTH/PULLBACK LATTICE (intermediate fields F(psi) of F(X)), of which the Dih_n-invariant subfields are the group special case — completeness now by LUROTH'S THEOREM (every intermediate field is F(psi)): closure by classification at the correct level. E35's empty band survives with the enlarged conclusion: abundant flats are pullback-structured (symmetric OR not); primitive stay <= 3.

nodes.title: THE CORRECTED LIST TARGET (X-4 verdict, verified): staircase split + primitive bound
nodes.statement: The unqualified worst-word exact-list bound is FALSE (verified counterexample: the quotient-coset staircase — locators L_B(X) G(X^M), M | n, M > t, S_Q = tail B plus h full M-cosets; top t+1 coefficients Q-independent so differences are codewords; ExactList >= C(n/M - 1, h), = 2^82.97 at the Row-C toy point vs 16n^3 = 2^34, pairwise overlaps < k throughout). THE CORRECTED STATEMENT: ExactList_C(w, A) <= QuotientStaircase(n,k,t) + DihedralStaircase-analogue + PrimitiveExactList, where QuotientStaircase = the explicit sum over M | n, M > t of coset-staircase terms (exact binomials), and the primitive target is PrimitiveExactList <= n^2 at corridor rows after stripping locators of the forms L_B(X) G(X^M) and X^e L_B(X) G(X^M + zeta X^-M). Transported quotient-row version identical (needed by TR's per-leaf term). MCA-side damage bounded: R_post counts SLOPES, and staircase supports share slopes (v8), so the compiler survives; the split is load-bearing for the LIST challenge and TR arithmetic. At prize rows M > t ~ 2^33 forces n/M <= 256, so staircase terms ~ C(128..256, 32..65) ~ 2^105-115 — under B* but far above 16n^3: QA.22 must re-run the budget with the staircase column. FOUR-COLUMN AMENDMENT (X-4b verdict, witness verified): the split gains the MomentTradeStaircase column — the primitive moment-null blocks are a THIRD cancellation mechanism outside all symmetry; see moment_trade_staircase and x4b_moment_trade_exclusion. The n^2 primitive target now applies AFTER the moment column.
nodes.attack_surface: the staircase terms are EXACT binomials (chargeable by census); the primitive bound post-strip is the new deep target — the strip removes precisely the coefficient-cancellation mechanism, so a primitive list member's locator has full coefficient spread: quantify via the eliminant/Wronskian of the locator differences

nodes.notes: QA.22: the staircase columns are AFFORDABLE at every candidate — the split's arithmetic viability is now verified, not expected. | FLIP on replay (Codex wave 3): TARGET -> CONDITIONAL. | WIRING FIX (amber-leaf audit, user catch #5): packet predicate u1_pullback_dichotomy was not wired req — promoted.

nodes.title: QA.23: the Mersenne wild rows — Dickson taxonomy + window arithmetic
nodes.statement: For the admissible wild rows (n in {2^13, 2^17, 2^19, 2^31}, q = (2^m - 1)^{2s} < 2^256): (a) enumerate exactly which (n, q, coset) pairs are wild (cosets of mu_n inherit wildness by scaling conjugacy); (b) the quotient taxonomy there = the Dickson subgroup lattice of PGL_2(F_{n-1}) (Borel, cyclic, dihedral, A_4, S_4, A_5, subfield PSL/PGL) — each subgroup class a candidate quotient stratum; (c) the unsafe side GAINS: more symmetry = more window families at wild rows — recompute the census window arithmetic there (thresholds may differ from tame rows; the determination is per-row, so wild rows get their own richer analysis, not a program failure); (d) the weight-2 inverse and completeness statements gain wild-row cases with Dickson conclusion spaces.
nodes.attack_surface: pure arithmetic for (a); Dickson's classical theorem for (b); the existing census machinery transported for (c); toy laboratory: F_49/mu_8 (fully computable wild row)
nodes.falsifier: an invariant structure at a wild toy row outside the Dickson-derived strata (F_49/mu_8 is exhaustively checkable)
nodes.notes: QA.23 (16/16): the wild-row arithmetic CLOSED (exact admissible list) + the F_49/mu_8 toy fully audited (Dickson strata + window arithmetic); the prize-scale Dickson budget theorem honestly left open — needed only if a wild row is ever submitted, and the family's tame rows suffice for the three-rate campaign. | QA.23 detail (#17): 26 admissible wild rows enumerated exactly; the F_49/mu_8 Dickson toy shows NO escape, but Dickson-derived window PARTITIONS need their own census column at wild rows (unsafe side bookkeeping, queued with the dossier). | PROVED on replay (Codex audit packet, 16 QA.23 checks PASS).

nodes.title: THE FIFTH MECHANISM (X-4b verdict, VERIFIED): primitive moment-trade / PTE staircases
nodes.statement: Cancellation is NOT symmetry: any disjoint family of t-moment-null blocks (e_1(B) = ... = e_t(B) = 0, |B| > t) generates exact-list staircases exactly as the quotient/dihedral ones do — the block locators have zero top-t sub-leading coefficients, so unions share top coefficients and differences drop to degree < k. VERIFIED PRIMITIVE WITNESS: B = {11^e : e in {0,1,2,4,16,45,50,60}} in mu_64 over F_193 — power sums vanish for r = 1,2,3 (r = 4 gives 18), locator top-3 sub-leading coefficients literally zero, NO rotation or reflection stabilizer (all independently recomputed). Replicated across cosets: post-strip exact lists >= C(R, R/2), exponential in n — the quotient/dihedral strip provably misses it. CODING IDENTITY: t-moment-null blocks are 0/1-coefficient dual codewords with t leading zero syndromes — sparse dual words at weights ABOVE every census we ran (E9/E30/E35 covered weight <= 4; structural blindness, honest lesson). The symmetry classes are the special case where invariance forces moment-nullity; the general class is Prouhet-Tarry-Escott-flavored and p-SPECIFIC.
nodes.attack_surface: existence per (n, p, t, b) is a classical-flavored finite-field question (0/1 words in cyclic-code duals with prescribed leading zeros) — CHECKABLE per official row (the Reading-B certification engine hook); E37 censuses toy and official-like rows
nodes.falsifier: n/a for the class (witness verified); the per-row EXCLUSION statements are the falsifiable targets
nodes.notes: THE UNIFICATION (matching-composition witness + fiber arithmetic, verified): t-moment-null blocks of size b ARE fibers of polynomials with zero top-t coefficients (elementary symmetric functions of a fiber = the map's coefficients, invariant in the fiber value below e_b), and the matching-composition counterexample's psi-fiber pairs are the same class at b = 2. The fifth mechanism = PULLBACK ALONG RATIONAL MAPS with coefficient constraints; the symmetry quotients are its invertible/group special cases. Trade FAMILIES mix maps across blocks (each block a fiber of its own map) — the family class is the mixed-pullback closure. | FLIP on replay (Codex wave 3): PROVABLE -> PROVED.

nodes.title: THE p-SPECIFIC EXCLUSION: no primitive moment trades at official rows (or charge them)
nodes.statement: For each official row (p, n, t): either (a) prove no super-logarithmic disjoint family of primitive (non-quotient, non-dihedral) t-moment-null blocks exists in the row domain — then the four-column split's moment column is empty/log-bounded there; or (b) enumerate/charge the existing families as the MomentTradeStaircase column with exact counts. Includes the full PTE-trade version (pairs P, Q with matching first-t power sums). This is per-prime certification-shaped — exactly the crux-2 architecture: the engine checks rows; Reading B semantics apply. The four-column split: ExactList <= QuotientStaircase + DihedralStaircase + MomentTradeStaircase + PrimitiveNoTradeExactList (<= n^2 target for the last).
nodes.attack_surface: per-row search = lattice/coding computation (0/1 dual words with t leading zeros, weight window (t, O(log n)]): MITM/BKZ machinery from the certifier pipeline applies directly; theory side: weight distributions of the relevant cyclic codes, Weil-type bounds may kill small blocks at large p (the F_193 witness needed small p relative to n)
nodes.falsifier: E37 finds primitive blocks at an official-row-like (p, n, t) with super-log families (then branch (b) is forced and the column gets charged)
nodes.notes: Reframed by the unification: the per-row question is 'which polynomial-fiber block families (zero top-t coefficient maps) exist in the row domain' — E37's census language updated; the Luroth frame may make the exclusion provable by degree arithmetic at large p. | U2 — THE LARGE-PRIME CONJECTURE (branch (a) sharpened with first-moment arithmetic): at official rows, primitive t-moment-null blocks of size b in (t, polylog n] do not exist — expected count ~ n^b p^{-t}, and t log2 p >= 384 dwarfs b log2 n <= 41b at every official row (the verified witness needed p ~ n^3 — tiny; official p ~ 2^128+). Route: Weil/character-sum bounds on the block variety. E37 tests the threshold empirically; if confirmed, the moment column is EMPTY at official rows and the exclusion is a provable-shaped theorem, not a certification burden. | X-5 DEFINITIONAL FIX: the moment/PTE column must carry a BOUNDED GRAMMAR (shared-map fiber families with exact counting rules), NOT arbitrary trades — arbitrary trades are universal by the star-PTE lemma and uncountable as a column. QA.22's computed columns are already bounded-grammar instances (shared-map quotient/dihedral) — affordable; the compression conjecture says the full dictionary stays affordable. | F2/E37 (#21, 9/9 replayed): THE THRESHOLD IS MEASURED — primitive t=3 blocks (b <= 8) exist ONLY at very small p (hits at p = 193/257/577 for n = 64; none from p = 641 up; none at representative n^2, n^3, or 2^61 scales). Official rows (p ~ 2^128+) are far beyond every observed hit. b = 9, 10 honestly left open. U2 survives with its Weil-route target inequality now empirically calibrated. | U2 PROMOTED TO PROOF TARGET (X-6 brief drafted): the Weil-route theorem over the full b-window (t, O(log n)], uniform — the census's b = 9,10 gap makes window uniformity mandatory in the proof. | U2 REFUTED AS UNIVERSAL (X-6, witness verified here: 234-bit prime, sparse relation 1+a+a^2+a^4 = 0, ord(a) = 1024, block {+-1, +-b, +-b^2, +-b^4} in mu_2048 over F_{p^2} — 3-moment-null, non-quotient, non-dihedral). MITIGATIONS, both verified: (1) the witness is INADMISSIBLE (q = 2^468 > 2^256 field cap); (2) the mechanism is resultant-divisibility — sparse-relation blocks exist EXACTLY at primes dividing fixed integers Res(relation, Phi_n) (finitely many exceptional primes per relation), so E37's small-p hits were small resultant factors and the 2^61 no-hits mean those primes divide no relevant resultant — evidence of SPARSITY, not a universal threshold. THE LANE'S NEW FORM: u2_per_row_moment_certifier — enumerate sparse-relation/moment ideals for b <= C log n, verify official-row primes divide none of the norms (pure divisibility checks; crux-2's architecture; Reading B semantics). The universal-theorem route is closed; the certification route is fully specified and cheap per row. Falsification #7 (mine). | THE CLOSURE PLAN (three pieces, window arithmetic first): (A) WINDOW SPLIT — blocks need b > t, so the small-block window (t, C log n] is EMPTY at prize-max rates (t ~ 10^9 >> log n): the sparse-relation class cannot exist there; small blocks threaten only Row-C-class rows (t = 3-5, b <= ~20). (B) SMALL-WINDOW CERTIFIER (Row-C-class, feasible NOW): per official row, certify no primitive t-null block of size b <= C log n exists in that row's domain — method: elimination/Groebner on the t power-sum equations over mu_n (b <= 20 variables), or the resultant route (enumerate sparse-relation patterns of weight <= b, check p divides no norm); E37's MITM machinery prototypes this; output = exact per-row certificates (Reading B inputs). (C) GIANT REGIME (prize-max, b >= t+1 ~ 10^9): disjointness caps gadget families at R <= n/t ~ 255; existing giant blocks are plausibly ONLY 2-power coset unions (M > t forces M >= 2^34 — the already-charged quotient staircase, QA.22-affordable); primitive-giant nonexistence is a named structural sub-target — and the FALLBACK is absorbency: even a charged primitive-giant column of QA.22-comparable size fits the 27-61-bit margins. Assign: (A)+(B) Codex; (C) statement first (roadmap-maker), then GPT Pro. | U2-C DRAFTED (u2c_giant_block_statement.md): the two-exit giant-regime statement with four handles, incl. the NEW complement duality (L_B L_comp = X^n - gamma forces co-blocks t-null — the regime is pinched from both ends) and the scaled toy falsifier (n = 256, t = 16 — trade-level MITM search). | F2-EXT (#25, 5/5 replayed): b = 9,10 CLOSED — low-prime hits persist (n=64, b=10 to p=1601); representative n^2/n^3/2^61 rows primitive-clean; the threshold picture holds at every tested b. | U2-C FALSIFIER SCAN: PASS (agent-green; my replay running in background — amend if red). Structural bonus: t-null blocks are BCH-type 0/1 words (DFT vanishing at 1..t); 2-power dictionary blocks reduce to residue classes mod n/16 (stride test); weight not divisible by 16 => automatically primitive. Both scales: only mu_16-cosets found; THE TAME DOOR DOES NOT OPEN (at n | q-1 the only minimal 2-power-root relation is 1 + (-1) = 0 — X-6's mechanism recurses into cosets). U2-C is PROOF-READY per the pre-registered exit; honest residuals: n=64 weights 19-31 and n=128 sampled not certified; special resultant primes untested. | QA.25 (#34): the boundary-scale ceilings are 1/1/64/1/1/2 across the six campaign rows — adding to QA.22 totals still fits every row. The boundary column is closed arithmetic at the candidates. | U2C-PRIME (#35): the repaired M >= t classifier charges every X-8 toy boundary witness — the residual dichotomy U2-C-prime machinery is validated. | FLIP on replay (Codex wave 3): TARGET -> CONDITIONAL.

The remaining obligation: bound post-strip rich points from affine nets. TWO candidate routes: (a) the nets may be chargeable to the UNIFIED pullback class (affine maps are degree-1 rational maps; net directions = mixed low-degree pullbacks — check against the Luroth frame before inventing new machinery); (b) if genuinely primitive, this is finite-field incidence geometry (Szemeredi-Trotter/Vinh-type rich-point bounds) — classical tools exist. NOTE the theme: affine/additive structure surfacing for the third time today (the involution witness, the moment blocks, now nets).

Else route (b): finite-field incidence bounds at the exact parameters (rich points of line arrangements over F_q, t+1-rich, poly-many lines)

Checkable at toys in an afternoon (net detector vs fiber dictionary); if true, the rich-line cap completes via P1's proved reduction + the unified strip. | U3 PROVED (F3, #20, 33/33 replayed): the affine-net residue CHARGES to mixed b=2 degree-1 pullback cells under the unified strip — 14 net cases over F_193 incl. the original P3 net (342 rich points, 726 pair trades, 4314 local identities, all verified). The unification's fourth guise confirmed absorbed.

nodes.title: U1 — THE CLOSING CONJECTURE: cancellation is pullback, quantitatively
nodes.statement: RESTATED (X-5 verdict — the tautology repaired): U1 = THE COMPRESSION THEOREM u1_pte_trade_compression. By the star-PTE lemma (PROVED), every same-top-t family is a star of canonical order-t PTE trades from any base — representation is free; the conjecture is COMPRESSION: if |F| > n^2, all but <= n^2 canonical trades belong to a FINITE CHARGEABLE PULLBACK DICTIONARY (data: finitely many rational maps psi_alpha, fiber values, fixed tails, zero-top-t coefficient constraints, and an EXACT staircase counting rule — the bounded-complexity grammar W3 must define). Quotient-row transport verbatim (the GAP-1 per-leaf form). EVIDENCE: six documented demolition attacks (cross-block compensation, defect-plus-point, mixed sizes, poles, towers, char-p switch nets) all die into canonical trades or rational-pullback variants — the strongest pre-proof evidence compression can have. RESIDUAL RED-TEAM TEMPLATE (dangerous for the narrow form): characteristic-p switch nets — many disjoint identical-defect gadgets with p-fold switches; no verified tame-row instance yet; F4 probe hunts them.

Two named gaps: alpha (sporadic-core bookkeeping) and beta (general-h via the correspondence version). X-7 = complete this skeleton.

nodes.title: PROVED (X-5): the star-PTE normal form — no non-PTE cancellation exists
nodes.statement: For any family of monic degree-A locators with roots in D sharing top t coefficients, and any base member S0: every member S decomposes canonically as S = C u P, S0 = C u Q with |P| = |Q| >= t+1 (unless S = S0) and e_r(P) = e_r(Q) for r <= t. Proof: L_S - L_S0 = L_C (L_P - L_Q); the degree constraint forces the top-t agreement; h <= t forces L_P = L_Q. Exact, scale-free, transports verbatim to quotient rows. CONSEQUENCE: there is no non-PTE cancellation mechanism at the two-locator level — any residual hardness is COMPRESSION of many trades, not representation.
nodes.notes: PROVENANCE: GPT Pro X-5 (demolition brief), verified here. Also the tautology catch: unrestricted PTE stripping makes literal U1 trivially true (remainder <= 1) — my U1 wording had a definitional flaw that would have made a 'proof' vacuous; caught pre-investment.

nodes.title: PROVED (X-6): the characteristic-zero lifting theorem
nodes.statement: If char(F_q) = p > max_{r <= t} C(b,r)^phi(n), then every b-subset of mu_n (2-power n) with e_1 = ... = e_t = 0 is a union of mu_M-cosets, M = 2^ceil(log2(t+1)) > t — quotient-staircase charged; if M does not divide b, no such block exists. UNIFORM IN b. Proof: mod-p vanishing lifts to characteristic zero above the norm bound; char-0 e_1 = 0 with 0/1 indicator forces Phi_n | P_B hence antipodal closure B = -B; locator descends to Q_1(X^2); iterate. Verified here. The threshold (~phi(n) log2 C(b,r) bits — thousands) is far above 2^128: correct but not the requested bound.
nodes.notes: PROVENANCE: GPT Pro X-6. The elegant core: antipodal descent — each moment level halves the domain; the 2-power structure does the rest.

nodes.title: BANKABLE PARTIAL (X-7): per-core sporadic fiber bound
nodes.statement: For h = t+1 and fixed core Q: non-v1 full-fiber trades P (L_P = L_Q - c) number <= poly(h) n^{2/3}, unless psi_Q's non-diagonal fiber product has a toral component (the structured branch). Rests on verified subgroup-points imports: Corvaja-Zannier positive-characteristic gcd estimate + Makarychev-Vyugin subgroup solution bounds; size hypotheses hold at official rows with >53 bits of margin (n <= 2^41 < p^{3/4}/3 at p >= 2^128; error term 12n^2/p < 2^{-42}).
nodes.notes: X-7 partial. CONDITIONAL on the no-toral hypothesis being the right split (T classifies the toral branch).

nodes.title: INPUT T: the toral fiber-product classification
nodes.statement: deg psi < p, D = gamma mu_n, p coprime to n: if psi's non-diagonal fiber product has a toral component x^a y^b = c, or psi has > poly(deg) n^{2/3} full D-fibers, then psi is v1-chargeable (monomial/power or Dickson/Chebyshev pullback, up to linear conjugacy, coset scaling, bounded tails). STRONGER than Ritt's Second Theorem (which IS verified: tame composition collisions of coprime degrees are monomial-or-Dickson, von zur Gathen normal forms); the needed form is a Laurent/toral statement — Zieve's Laurent-polynomial decompositions are the right orbit, unverified at this exact strength. CORRECTED WORDING (per X-7): Frobenius/additive wild composition is excluded by deg < p; tame exceptional families (monomial, Dickson) are the DICTIONARY, not absent phenomena. X-8 CORRECTIONS: (i) the exact-polynomial toral case is PROVED and gives MONOMIAL ONLY (t_poly_exact_toral) — Dickson requires the Laurent/rational case, so the load-bearing missing theorem is a POSITIVE-CHARACTERISTIC LAURENT-RITT statement (named; Zannier's polynomial Ritt II does not cover it); (ii) the many-fiber threshold MUST carry the n^2/p term: the trigger is K > C(d) max{n^{2/3}, n^2/p}, equivalently the hypotheses need p >= C(d) n^{4/3} — SATISFIED at the six candidates by ~200 bits (p ~ 2^250 vs n^{4/3} = 2^54.7) but low-q admissible rows are NOT covered (the same low-q danger zone as the boundary column); (iii) the bounded-tails wrapper is a named missing lemma (fiber-minus-B-points incidence loss). T = one proved subtheorem + conditional assembly on {Laurent-Ritt, corrected threshold, tails lemma}.
nodes.attack_surface: literature verification first (Zieve, Laurent decompositions; Bilu-Tichy orbit); else prove directly: a toral component gives a multiplicative identity between root multisets of psi - y — push through the tame normal forms
nodes.falsifier: a tame-degree psi with toral fiber-product component that is NOT monomial/Dickson-conjugate (searchable at toy degrees)
nodes.notes: LITERATURE SEARCH RESOLVED THE ROUTE (roadmap-lane, web-verified to exist; exact statements to be pulled from the PDFs before use): (1) Zannier, 'Ritt's Second Theorem in arbitrary characteristic', Crelle 445 (1993) 175-203 — Ritt II in char p under tameness; (2) Kulkarni-Muller-Sury, 'Quadratic factors of f(X) - g(Y)' — Bilu's classification EXTENDED TO ARBITRARY CHARACTERISTIC (the bidegree-(1,1) toral case directly); (3) Turnwald's Dickson/Schur treatment for the Dickson normal forms; (4) Fried-Guralnick-Saxl + Klyachko for the exceptional direction (degree coprime to p: compositions of monomials/Dicksons exhaust). THE ASSEMBLY: toral factor x^a y^b = c => monomial substitution => Laurent functional equation => Zannier char-p Ritt II + Turnwald => monomial/Dickson. THE COMPLICATION FOUND: Zieve's 'Decompositions of Laurent polynomials' (the natural one-shot import) is CHARACTERISTIC 0 (built on Bilu-Tichy) — cannot be imported directly; the route goes through Zannier + substitution instead. THE TWO SEAMS THAT ARE OURS: (a) lifting general bidegree (a,b) toral factors to the quadratic/KMS case via the substitution (elementary-looking, needs writing); (b) the bounded-tails robustness wrapper. Status upgraded TARGET -> PROVABLE: an assembly task with named sources, not an open import. | T IS NOW ASSEMBLED MODULO ONE LEMMA: toral branch PROVED (t_laurent_ritt_toral_stabilizer — cyclic/dihedral pullback exactly); many-fibers branch = the verified subgroup-point bound with the corrected max{n^{2/3}, n^2/p} threshold (satisfied at the candidates by ~200 bits) forces a toral component, then the theorem applies; REMAINING: the bounded-tails wrapper (fibers-minus-B-points incidence loss — lemma-sized, named in X-8). Status TARGET -> CONDITIONAL on the tails wrapper. GRAMMAR NOTE: v1's charged forms should read outer-LAURENT in the cyclic branch per the X-9 correction.

nodes.title: INPUT A: the active-core incidence theorem (the genuine new math)
nodes.statement: Sum over cores Q subset S0 (|Q| = t+1) of the sporadic full-fiber counts K_Q^sp is <= n^2. NOT bookkeeping: the corrected fiber-product count is LINEAR in K (fibers pair only within themselves — K h(h-1), not K^2 h^2; the skeleton's quadratic count was wrong, falsification #8), so per-core bounds give n^{2/3} each and the summation over active cores is a genuine incidence theorem on the PTE incidence variety e_r(P) = e_r(Q), r <= h-1, P subset D, Q subset S0.
nodes.attack_surface: second-moment/energy count over (Q, P) incidences; or show > n^{4/3} active cores force shared toral structure; toy calibration via H1's harness data (which already measures active cores implicitly)
nodes.falsifier: a toy base with active-core mass exceeding the n^2 budget post-strip (extend H1's harness with a per-core column — cheap)
nodes.notes: P-A DATA (#27, 28/28): active cores are RARE — max 6 active cores per base, max K_Q = 2, budget ratio 0.016 of n^2 at toys. THE DECOMPOSITION SEED: A splits as (A-i) the active-core rarity lemma — a core is active only if some full fiber of psi_Q lies in D, itself a strongly suppressed event; count E[active] directly — and (A-ii) the per-core cap (ALREADY BANKED as u1_minimal_fixed_core_sporadic_bound). The incidence-variety machinery may be unnecessary: rarity x per-core cap could give the n^2 budget outright. | X-10: A reduces EXACTLY to anchored_nontoral_pte_bound (the orbit argument proved; the anchored count is the resisting sub-count). The toy tables match P-A (in-range rows tiny; low-q rows blow up per the verified falsifier). | FLIP APPLIED on my replay (Codex rednode batch, 2026-07-04): TARGET -> CONDITIONAL; referee packet in nodes/u1_alpha_active_core_incidence/conditional.md (extracted surgically — Codex's branch base predated the Pro packet and expansions; wholesale merge would have deleted them).

nodes.title: INPUT B: the band-trade reduction
nodes.statement: For h in (t+1, (log2 n)^2]: every family of > n^2 non-v1 canonical band trades (deg(L_P - L_Q) <= h-t-1) satisfies one of: (1) contains > n^2 minimal subtrades after bounded-tail deletion; (2) the moving pencil L_Q + f lies in a v1-chargeable pullback linear series; (3) already moment/PTE-charged. The correspondence route fails as-is (moving curves: psi_P, psi_Q vary per trade — curve-point theorems need fixed curves or a clustering statement).
nodes.attack_surface: the linear-series view (bounded-degree pencils of divisors of X^n - delta with many totally-split members); or a difference/derivative step down in h; toy census of band trades at n = 64 calibrates which exit dominates
nodes.falsifier: a toy band-trade family avoiding all three exits (searchable with H1 machinery extended to h > t+1)
nodes.notes: P-B CENSUS (#29, 93/93): PASS — no toy family avoids the three exits; every canonical dihedral-orbit representative routed. B's three-exit structure is confirmed observed structure; the proof should follow the census's routing table. | B-WRITEUP (#37): exits 1 and 2 REDUCE FORMALLY (proved reductions); exit 3 is EXACTLY the X-10 residue (anchored_nontoral_pte_bound + the defect/tails wrapper). B collapses into the final estimate — status CONDITIONAL on it. THE CONVERGENCE COMPLETES: A, B, both active-shadow bounds, and the tails wrapper are now all faces of A_h^nt.

Consumers: the assembled staircase (A1) and the graded tangent ledger bound (A2) — one machinery, two clients. E33's occupancy data calibrates (observed: totals stayed linear).

Same pattern as OCC-2 and everything else in this program: raw/formal fails, active/post-strip holds. | FLIP on replay (Codex rednode wave 2): TARGET -> CONDITIONAL; packet in nodes/a1_lower_overlap_occupied_subcore_accounting/. | WIRING FIX (amber-leaf audit, user catch #5): packet predicate anchored_nontoral_pte_bound was not wired req — promoted.

nodes.title: U2-C (PROOF-READY): the giant-regime dichotomy
nodes.statement: At official prize-max rows (b >= t+1 ~ 1e9; the small window is empty by piece-A arithmetic): every t-null block is a disjoint union of full 2-power cosets (charged, QA.22-affordable) — or the absorbency fallback (primitive giant blocks countable with star-mass under the 2^27 margin). Full statement: u2c_giant_block_statement.md. FALSIFIER SCAN PASSED (both scales, the tame door does not open: the only minimal 2-power-root relation at n | q-1 is 1 + (-1) = 0 — the X-6 mechanism recurses into cosets). SUSPECTED PROOF SKELETON: adapt the char-0 antipodal-descent argument (u2_large_characteristic_lift) directly in F_q with the scan's minimal-relation fact as base case, in the BCH frame (t-null = t leading DFT zeros). X-8 brief.
nodes.attack_surface: the antipodal-descent adaptation; the complement duality pinches from both ends; the BCH/stride frame makes the coset classification a one-line test
nodes.falsifier: scan residuals: n=64 weights 19-31 exhaustive extension; certified n=128 run; the special resultant primes
nodes.notes: REFUTED AS STATED (X-8 task 1, verified here incl. the Pocklington certificate): the quotient zero-sum construction — B_S = preimage of a zero-sum antipodal-free subset S of mu_R under x -> x^t (R = n/t) — is t-null, NOT a union of M > t cosets, and subset-sum pigeonhole gives >= 2^{R/2}/q of them. Falsification #9 (mine). THE STRUCTURAL RE-ANALYSIS THAT SOFTENS IT: B_S IS a coset union — at the BOUNDARY scale M = t, which the dichotomy excluded by requiring M > t. The class is exactly characterizable (zero-sum quotient patterns) and countable (subset-sum collision counts) => CHARGEABLE, not chaos. THE TRIGGER LOCALIZES THE DAMAGE (verified arithmetic): q < 2^{R/2} needed; at the six campaign candidates (q ~ 2^250+): ~2^6 blocks at rate-1/4-shape, ~0 at t = 2^33 rows; the danger zone is MID-RANGE admissible q. SCAN-BLINDNESS LESSON (third instance): the toy scan could not see this — R/2 = 4 at toys vs 2^31 primes made the trigger unreachable; parameter-regime blindness is intrinsic to scaled toys; adversarial construction found it again.

Obligation = procedure correctness + a calibration demonstration; per-exhibit execution is the submitter's compute burden under Reading B. U2-B DEFERRED by Codex for honest memory reasons (Row-C b <= 100 exceeds current MITM reach): needs either the resultant route (low-memory) or SOLO scheduling with the elimination method — respec before dispatch. | DISPATCH SKIP = A FINDING: the certifier's own fail-closed rule needs a pinned Row-C prime — but official_row_primes_pinning (PROVED) says NO fixed primes exist. Procedure defined + proved-correct; TOY EXEMPLAR shipped (N=16 both verdicts, exhaustive). Prize cells = dim-64/128 LATTICE enumeration (fplll-feasible, NOT a theorem) — needs the pinned exhibit cell; answer may be collision-found (survivable). Tier-0 CONFIRMED: the remaining work is a lattice computation. | LATTICE RUN LAUNCHED (fpylll, Modal rs-mca-lattice-cert): exact-SVP verdict at 4 cells incl. the REAL dim-64 Row-C cell (250-bit stand-in prime, q=1 mod 128). CERTIFIED iff lambda_1 > 2 sqrt(N/2); validation cells (N=16 certified / N=32 collision) confirm the pipeline. Result closes the node by machine-checkable computation OR returns a survivable collision. | NOT closed by the e1 lattice run: a primitive t-null block is the MULTI-MOMENT (codim-t) object (sum_x v_x x^m = 0, m=1..t), not the codim-1 e1 collision. SAME methodology (fold + sparse-vector enumeration) on the higher-codimension lattice L_t = {v : sum v_x x^m = 0 mod p, m=1..t}, seeking a 0/1 vector of support b <= C log n. Needs a SEPARATE run once the exact Row-C small-t and C are pinned from the exhibit spec. Feasible, same tooling. | PROVED by machine-checkable computation (Reading B, parallel to integer_code_distance_cert): the t=3 moment-trade MITM census. GATE VALIDATED — reproduces the F2 census EXACTLY: n=64 primes 257->192 / 577->64 primitive blocks (F2's recorded hits), 641/769 CERTIFIED (F2's 'clean at 641+'). MITM is exhaustive/complete per cell. Depends on U2-A window arithmetic (PROVED, 174/174). Verifier: notes/modal_u2_census.py.

nodes.title: THE REPAIR: the boundary-scale (M = t) zero-sum column + crossover arithmetic (QA.25)
nodes.statement: (1) DICTIONARY CLAUSE: mu_t-coset unions with zero-sum value patterns (and the multi-level generalization for non-2-power t*: zero sums at each multiple of M <= t*) join the charged classes — exactly characterized, exactly countable (subset-sum collision counts, ~max(0, 2^{R/2}/q) scale). (2) QA.25 CROSSOVER ARITHMETIC: per admissible row, the boundary-column staircase mass vs B* = q/2^128 — the count GROWS as q shrinks while B* SHRINKS: locate the crossover row exactly; above it (incl. all six campaign candidates, verified nearly-empty) the column fits; below it the rows need richer per-row analysis (possibly a new determined-region-style boundary in q). (3) The residual dichotomy conjecture (U2-C'): every t-null block is a union of mu_M-cosets with M >= t... M = t INCLUDED... with zero-sum patterns — i.e., the boundary class + the old dictionary EXHAUST t-null blocks. The X-8 construction is consistent with this; its falsifier is a t-null block that is not even a boundary-scale coset union.

nodes.notes: (B) DECOMPOSED (roadmap-lane): B1 the char-0 giant coset theorem — PROVED (one-paragraph Galois/Fourier argument, exhaustively toy-verified); B2a the boundary class — exactly counted (QA.25 fits); B2b the mod-p extras bound — the residue, with the Frobenius gap explaining WHY it exists, the first-moment balance explaining why counting can't close it, and the 123-bit cushion changing its difficulty class (no-concentration, not exclusion). (B) is now structurally parallel to (A): char-0 classification proved + one mod-p gap lemma — but WITHOUT the universal-variety trick (2^41 variables), compensated by the cushion. | FLIP on replay (Codex wave 3): TARGET -> CONDITIONAL.

nodes.title: PROVED (X-8 task 2): exact polynomial toral components give power-pullback only
nodes.statement: char K = 0 or > deg psi: if psi(X) - psi(Y) has a non-diagonal toral component in (G_m)^2, the component is X = lambda Y and psi = phi(X^m) (m = ord(lambda) > 1) after scaling — the monomial case ONLY. Proof: sign/degree bookkeeping kills X^A Y^B = c (positive exponents vs negative); X^A = c Y^B forces A = B = 1; psi(lambda Y) = psi(Y) forces lambda^i = 1 on all exponents. Verified here (elementary). CONSEQUENCE: Dickson/Chebyshev does NOT arise from polynomial toral components — it needs Laurent/rational psi, so T's Laurent case is load-bearing, not decorative.
nodes.notes: X-8 deliverable; quotient-row stable. Citations upgraded to VERIFIED-AGAINST-SOURCE by GPT Pro for: KMS quadratic factors (incl. char-p wild-case exclusions), vzGathen/Zannier Ritt II Fact 3.3, Turnwald Thm 1 (tame <=> deg < p), Corvaja-Zannier-via-Makarychev-Vyugin (WITH the essential max{n^{2/3}, 12 n^2/p} form).

nodes.title: PROVED (X-9): the tame Laurent-Ritt / toral stabilizer theorem — T's core
nodes.statement: k algebraically closed, psi a Laurent polynomial of degree d, char 0 or p > d: if the reduced non-diagonal fiber product has a geometric toral component, then EITHER psi = F(x^m), m > 1, F LAURENT (F in k[z, 1/z]; F in k[z] if psi is an ordinary polynomial) — the cyclic branch — OR psi = F(x^m + alpha x^-m) with F in k[z] — the dihedral/Dickson branch. Converse holds. PROOF SELF-CONTAINED, ZERO IMPORTS (verified here incl. the dihedral factorization identity and the discriminant of the rational counterexample): (1) degree multiplicativity collapses every primitive toral component x^a y^b = c to y = lambda x or xy = c — the graph of a tame automorphism of G_m; (2) the toral stabilizer is finite (separability from p > d) and cyclic-or-dihedral; (3) invariant fields k(x^m) and k(x^m + alpha x^-m) + the Laurent pole argument pin the outer map. TWO WORDING CORRECTIONS to T as I posed it: the cyclic outer map is Laurent, not polynomial (witness x^m + x^-2m); general rational psi needs an outer RATIONAL map (witness R(z) = z + 1/(z^2+1), degree 3, no totally ramified fiber, discriminant 592). Scale-free; tame hypothesis survives every quotient row with ~2^100 slack.
nodes.notes: PROVENANCE: GPT Pro X-9 (10-for-10 substantive). The theorem is EASIER than full Laurent-Ritt precisely because the degree collapse kills general-bidegree components geometrically — the fence that made T look hard was an artifact of not parametrizing. | T-TOY (#32, 212/212): the classification confirmed empirically through degree 20 at tame toy rows — zero unclassified bidegree-(1,1) toral/affine cases; line factors are linear-conjugate power pullbacks; Dickson appears at the X^2 symmetry exactly as the theorem predicts.

Statement: the active shadow is budget-bounded; E33/P-A-style occupancy data says active objects are rare throughout this program.

nodes.title: THE FINAL ESTIMATE: A_h^nt <= hn — everything reduces to this
nodes.statement: H = mu_n in F_q*, n = 2^s, p >= n^2 (NEARLY SHARP — verified low-q falsifier below), 2 <= h <= (log2 n)^2. A_h^nt = #{anchored pairs (P, Q): 1 in P, disjoint h-subsets of H, e_j(P) = e_j(Q) for j < h, L_Q non-toral}. TARGET: A_h^nt <= hn. CONSEQUENCES (all proved conditional, X-10): #uncharged split pairs = (n/h) A_h^nt <= n^2 (the orbit argument: uncharged pairs have trivial scaling stabilizer, orbits of size n — PROVED); quotient-row transport verbatim; the k-fiber version via A_{h,k}^nt <= C_k hn; the tails wrapper via the defect version A_{h,B}^nt <= C_{h,B} n. WHY THE VERIFIED INPUTS STOP HERE (X-10's insufficiency ledger, source-checked): the Corvaja-Zannier curve theorem is one dimension too small (the system lives in (G_m)^{2h}, slicing loses n^{2h-O(1)}); per-psi caps do not sum over C(n,h) cores; the derivative identity (L_P' = L_Q') isolates the clustering but does not bound it; Shkredov-Vyugin additive-shift energy controls LINEAR patterns only, not h-1 simultaneous power sums. This is a high-dimensional finite-field PTE/Vinogradov mean-value problem for multiplicative subgroups — plausibly at the research frontier.
nodes.attack_surface: the derivative-clustering lens (all fibers of one antiderivative share L'); Vinogradov-style mean-value induction on h (each moment level should cost a factor ~n... the conjectured bound hn is LINEAR — the strongest possible form; a weaker n^{1+eps} or n polylog n version still closes the campaign with adjusted budgets); SP-CENSUS (running) measures A_h^nt directly at toys — add the anchored column
nodes.falsifier: SP-CENSUS anchored counts exceeding hn at any in-range (p >= n^2) toy row
nodes.notes: SP-CENSUS (#33): every uncharged excess sits at q << n^2 — empirical CONFIRMATION of X-10's low-q falsifier (F17/mu16 at 3.8x, F41/mu20 at 13.7x, F97/mu24 at 52x — all far below the q >= n^2 line); NO in-range excess observed. Follow-up: SP-CENSUS-2 at q > n^2 rows (the census's toy fields were all sub-threshold). The anchored/active framing note is the conservative reading; the sharp reading is that the hypothesis line is real and empirically visible. | X-11: status -> CONDITIONAL on active_core_count_bound (the reduction chain is proved: anchor injectivity + the per-core cap). The h = 2 rung is PROVED (delta = 1/3). The residue is one peel smaller: count active cores, not split pairs. | QA.26 THE TOLERANCE LADDER (#38, replayed green): THE REWIRING DISCOUNT — the raw row arithmetic (QA.22 + QA.25 certificates, exact integer room) tolerates a DIRECT compiler column of n^{alpha+1} with alpha_max = 2.0925 at every row (limiting: the prize rows), i.e. A_h^nt <= n^3 suffices IF the consumers charge the split-pair mass directly into the compiler budget as a column (like the staircases) instead of through the orbit-converted n^2 form. The n^2 requirement is an ARTIFACT of how U1/B's formal reductions currently route. The tails constraint alpha + lambda <= 1 also relaxes under rewiring. NOTE the elementary freeze-bound A_3^nt << n^3 already MEETS the rewired target at h = 3. NEW TASK W4: re-derive the two consumption points (U1's primitive column, B's exit 3) against the n^3 direct-column form — pure convention plumbing with a factor-n prize. | SP-CENSUS-2 (#39, 54/54): an in-range falsifier OF THE STRICT hn FORM under the CYCLIC-ONLY classifier — F1153/mu32 (q = 1.13 n^2, the boundary zone), h = 8: A_8 = 976 vs hn = 256. ANALYSIS: (i) pigeonhole predicts nothing at h = 8 => the mass is STRUCTURED; the classifier tested only the cyclic branch — dihedral/boundary/zero-sum classes live exactly at n = 32, h = 8; RECLASS-976 (queued) re-classifies against the full strip. (ii) EVEN IF genuinely uncharged: 976 < n^2 = 1024 (the ladder holds) and 976 << n^3 (the W4-rewired target is untouched). (iii) All other in-range rows CLEAR. The trophy hn dies at the boundary zone; the campaign never needed it. EXTREMAL-MINE (#40, 20/20): the 976 do NOT cluster into few derivative classes — the derivative route is dead for the boundary mass; the high-q + full-strip + ladder formulation is the survivor.

nodes.title: PROVED/VERIFIED (X-10): the low-q split-pair falsifier — p >= n^2 is nearly sharp
nodes.statement: At h = 2, split pairs are sum collisions (L_{a,b} determined by a+b, ab); charged pairs are EXACTLY the antipodal-sum ones (the fiber-product line X+Y = s is toral iff s = 0); pigeonhole gives uncharged > n^2 whenever q < ~n^2/7. VERIFIED EXACT INSTANCE: n = 64, p = 193: 18,816 uncharged vs n^2 = 4,096 (charged = 992 = 32*31 exactly, matching the antipodal count). The trigger decreases in h (h = 2 is worst), so p >= n^2 kills the mechanism at every h; official rows sit 48.8 bits above the h = 2 trigger. The X-8 pigeonhole lesson, miniaturized and now built into the theorem's hypotheses as a nearly-sharp condition.
nodes.notes: X-10 phase 1. Also verified: sparse-relation seeds do not amplify to uncharged split pairs above the threshold (pullback amplification is cyclically charged).

nodes.title: PROVED (X-11): the h = 2 anchored rung — A_2^nt <= 6n^(5/3)
nodes.statement: For h = 2 under p >= n^2: split pairs are sum collisions 1 + a = b + c; non-charged forces s = 1 + a != 0; for each of <= n anchors the pairs (b,c) lie on the line b + c = s, whose H^2-points are <= 12 n^{2/3} by the verified Corvaja-Zannier bound (chi = 1, the n^2/p term dead); total A_2^nt <= 6 n^{5/3} (2 n^{5/3} in prime fields via Garcia-Voloch). A real delta = 1/3 rung — 25-29 bits above the trophy at campaign scales, and the proof METHOD (freeze-to-a-curve + CZ) is the template for partial general-h bounds (elementary A_h^nt << n^{2h-3}).
nodes.notes: X-11 deliverable; verified here (the charged h=2 class is exactly antipodal-sum, matching the census's 992). NOTE: trade sizes start at h = t+1 >= 4 at the campaign rows, so this rung is a template and base case, not a direct consumer input.

nodes.title: THE RESIDUE, PEELED ONCE MORE (X-11): the active-core count C_h^nt
nodes.statement: THE TERMINAL NODE (the contract for the autonomous proof loop — read this in full). SETTING: H = mu_n in F_q*, n = 2^s, char p >= n^2, h in [3, (log2 n)^2] (h = 2 is EXCLUDED — it has its own proved bound A_2^nt <= 6n^{5/3}; its active-core count genuinely exceeds n^{1/3} shapes near q = n^2). OBJECT: anchored cores P (1 in P, |P| = h) whose locator L_P admits a second completely split H-fiber, with (P, Q) NOT charged by the FULL STRIP = {cyclic: psi = F(x^m), F Laurent, m > 1 (X-9); dihedral: psi = F(x^m + alpha x^-m) (X-9); boundary-scale zero-sum coset patterns (the #35 classifier); moment/PTE-block pullback forms (W3 grammar v1)}. TARGETS, weakest first (ANY rung closes the campaign): (L3) #uncharged split pairs <= n^3 per row — sufficient AFTER the W4 rewiring (QA.26: all six rows tolerate the direct compiler column at alpha = 2.09; W4 = re-derive the two consumption points, part of this goal); (L2) <= n^2 — sufficient for the proof stack as currently wired; (L1/trophy) C_h^nt <= poly(h) n^{1/3} — dead in strict form at the q ~ n^2 boundary under the cyclic-only classifier (the 976 at F1153/mu32 h=8 — RECLASS against the full strip is an open subtask of this goal), plausibly alive away from the boundary. HIGH-Q FORM (most promising): for q >= n^c, ANY c <= 6, the fully-stripped count VANISHES for h >= 3 — empirically true at c = 3 everywhere tested; official rows afford c = 6 with 4 bits of slack. PROVED TOOLS IN-REPO: the star-PTE normal form; anchor injectivity (derivative classes are injective); the per-core cap poly(h) n^{2/3} (CZ/MV, verified, p >= n^2 kills the n^2/p term); the X-9 toral classification (toral => cyclic or dihedral, self-contained); the h=2 rung's freeze-to-a-curve method (elementary A_h << n^{2h-3}); the h=3 (a,b,t) rational parametrization (three explicit rational functions, X-12 verdict — REPLICATE IN-REPO before building on it, Pro's census numbers 18/129 are unverified by our harness). KNOWN-DEAD ROUTES (do not respend): Weil/char sums (subgroup below the Weil floor); per-curve bounds summed over cores (diverges); quadratic VMVT import (n^{2h-3-1/9}); char-0 lifting at campaign scale (phi(n) = 2^40 exponents); naive resultant elimination (degree-n objects); elementary rigidity R1-R4 (measured dead, 147 cores); derivative clustering of boundary mass (#40). HYGIENE: one lemma per file/packet, honest labels (PROVED only with complete proof), every numeric claim verifier-backed, every conditional's hypothesis a named node; statuses flip only on the roadmap lane's replay.
nodes.attack_surface: Open routes, in promise order: (1) THE HIGH-Q VANISHING route — the empirical transition is bracketed (mass at q = 1.13n^2, zero at n^3); locate it finely (the q-sweep), then prove vanishing above it: candidate mechanisms — the (a,b,t) incidence at three simultaneous H-memberships (each costs n/q; at q >= n^3 the total parameter mass n^2 q (n/q)^3 = n^5/q^2 < 1: the challenge is converting this expectation into exclusion via the parametrization's algebraic structure, NOT pigeonhole); (2) the h = 3 case fully first (the parametrization makes it three rational functions — an exact component analysis of the incidence surface may be feasible symbolically), then induct via the freeze method with the h=3 base replacing the trivial base — recompute what n-power the induction then yields; (3) the full-strip cleanup (RECLASS-976): if the strip charges the boundary mass, restate and retarget; (4) W4 rewiring for the n^3 rung. nodes.title: PROVED (roadmap-lane, toy-verified): the char-0 giant coset theorem
nodes.statement: Over characteristic zero: every 0/1 t-null vector on mu_n (n = 2^s) is a union of mu_M-cosets with M > t. ONE-PARAGRAPH PROOF (Fourier/Galois): the exponent polynomial f_B has INTEGER coefficients, so f_B(zeta^r) = 0 propagates to the full Galois orbit of zeta^r — all frequencies of the same 2-adic valuation; r in [1, t] realizes every valuation v <= log2(t), so B-hat vanishes at ALL r not divisible by 2^{v+1}-max = the first 2-power > t; hence B-hat is supported on MZ (M = 2^{ceil log2 t}+... the least 2-power > t) and the 0/1 indicator equals its fiber average => a coset union. Verified exhaustively at (n, t) = (16, 4): exactly the mu_8-cosets and their union. NOTE: even easier than X24 (0/1 giant beats signed small-h); the X-8 boundary blocks are FINITE-FIELD-ONLY objects, consistent (see b2's Frobenius gap). STANDALONE PACKET SHIPPED (9/9): full proof written; verifier uses exact cyclotomic arithmetic — full 2^16 brute at (16,4) plus exact rational-nullspace proofs of both directions at (16,4)/(32,4)/(32,8) (nullity = n/M, counts = 2^{n/M}).

nodes.title: (B)'s residue: the mod-p extras bound at prize-max — with a 123-bit cushion
nodes.statement: At official prize-max rows: the count of non-coset-union t-null blocks (and their trade families) is <= n^3 = 2^123. STRUCTURE: (i) THE FROBENIUS GAP — q = 1 mod n makes Frobenius act trivially on frequencies, so B1's Galois forcing has NO mod-p analogue; extras genuinely can exist (the X-8 boundary class is exactly such) — the char-0/finite-field gap is fundamental, not technical. (ii) THE FIRST-MOMENT BALANCE: t log2 q ~ 2.15e12 vs log2(2^n) = 2.2e12 — the prize-max giant regime sits within ~2% of the counting threshold; pure counting can NEVER close it. (iii) THE CUSHION: the budget is 2^123, not 1 — the statement is 'no 2^123-fold concentration above the balanced mean', a FIXED arithmetic quantity per row (no adversarial quantifier). (iv) The boundary/zero-sum class is EXACTLY counted (QA.25: <= 64 per row, fits). Routes for the residue: the divisor frame (L_B = degree-b divisor of X^n - delta with a top-t coefficient gap — divisor-with-prescribed-coefficients counting), the mu_M-fiber descent (128 coupled fiber-DFTs), or a second-moment/pair-correlation bound at giant scale. SCAN VERDICT (39/39 rows, replayed): NOT FALSIFIED — and the shape is the best case: NO spike at the balance crossing; extras die BEFORE nominal balance, decaying faster than the mean; above balance every t-null block is exactly a coset union (the coset floor). Max observed excess 2^4.18 vs the toy cushion 2^15 — concentration exponent ~4 bits against the 123-bit prize cushion. NO SIXTH GUISE: every near-crossing survivor is X-8/QA.25 boundary-class. Coverage exact and honest: exhaustive mu_32 all-t, n=64 window b in [5,10] (amended from [5,16], disclosed), n=256/512 known-class arithmetic + falsifier-only sampling. RESIDUAL for the proof: large weights and prize scales rest on known-class arithmetic — the divisor-frame lemma must close them, now with the calibrated target: smooth sub-mean decay, boundary-class-only survivors, 119 bits of margin.

nodes.title: THE NEW BLOCKER: a scalable D(n,h) certificate at n = 1024
nodes.statement: The (A) closure's (C1) input cannot be computed by direct elimination (pilot-measured: dead at n = 32). NEEDED: a certificate for 'no non-toral mod-p solutions of the anchored system rem(X^n - 1, S^2 - S(1)^2) = 0' computable at n = 1024, h in the window, for the six official primes. ROUTES: (i) 2-ADIC RECURSIVE DESCENT (most promising): X^n - 1 = (X^{n/2} - 1)(X^{n/2} + 1) — the divisor S^2 - lambda distributes across the dyadic tower in finitely many degree patterns; per pattern the conditions decompose into subproblems at n/2 — D(n,h) as a product of much smaller resultants over descent patterns (the same tower structure that powers every 2-power argument in this campaign); (ii) mod-p elimination for the six specific primes (kills coefficient growth but symbolic degree growth in the 41 squarings remains — feasibility unclear); (iii) the absorbency retreat: even without certificates, bound the multiplicity at possibly-bad primes structurally (A3's G3 gap — currently no tool). Route (i) is the one with a campaign pedigree.
nodes.attack_surface: work out the descent pattern combinatorics at h = 4, n = 32 vs the pilot's computed data (the calibration case exists); if the recursion closes, the n = 1024 certificate is log2(n) = 10 descent levels of bounded work
nodes.falsifier: the descent producing cross-terms that do not decompose (check at n = 32 against the pilot's exact eliminant)
nodes.notes: DECOMPOSED (roadmap-lane, seed identity sympy-verified): C1a — direct MITM certificates at h = 4/5, n = 1024 (FEASIBLE NOW, ~2^35 hash entries; covers the most exposed window sizes); C1b — the descent-injection certificate (trades descend to band pairs via the exact pushforward identity, budget h/2 per level; only 4 levels needed to reach the censusable n = 64; the injection lemma is the one new statement, calibratable against the pilot's exact n = 32 ground truth); C1c — the antipodal branch tracked as paid per level (X24's mechanism); C1d — the interface with the h-window audit (running). ALSO NOTED: at official primes the first-moment gap is 700+ bits per h (unlike the giant regime's razor edge) — if C1b resists, a large-sieve/averaging argument over patterns is the theory fallback. | COVERAGE MAP after the delta-saturation correction: C1a (direct MITM) covers h = 4, maybe 5; C1b (descent) covers h <= ~8-10; h >= ~16 needs the large-h emptiness lemma (route ii) as a third piece IF the window audit's H_max reaches that high. The audit is the decisive pending input. | WINDOW VERDICT: H_max = A. Coverage now: C1a h=4(-5), C1b h<=~10, NEITHER for h in (10, A] — the widened gap is noded (midlarge_h_certification) and goes into the Fable brief. | FLIP on replay (Codex wave 6): TARGET -> CONDITIONAL — the assembly implication packaged; closes when the named frontier inputs land.

nodes.title: C1a: direct per-row certificates at the window's bottom (feasible NOW)
nodes.statement: At h = 4 (and plausibly 5): the per-row trade search at n = 1024 is FEASIBLE by MITM — pattern space C(1024, 8) ~ 2^55, halves ~ 2^{35} hash entries. Certify the official Row-C primes directly: no non-toral h=4 trades at (p, zeta). Pure compute; the exact analogue of the banked toy MITM machinery scaled up. Covers the window's most exposed sizes (the smallest h dominate risk). DELIVERED (27-check verifier green; 13/13 fast-mode replayed): (1) EXACT COMPLETE census over REAL finite fields at n = 16/32/64/128/256 — zero non-toral h=4 trades, toral exactly C(n/4,2) at every scale (extends the pilot's char-0 emptiness to actual F_p, two octaves further); (2) a decisive DETECTION gate: at the exceptional prime F17 the pipeline finds all 120 genuine non-toral 4-trades — sensitivity proved, and it caught+fixed a sign-convention bug that would have silently blinded the fast path; (3) n = 1024: machinery validated, toral family certified (32640), exhaustive spot-slice clean; the FULL run honestly deferred (~18h and ~16GB unbucketed; e_1-bucketing fits 2GB) — unconditional n=1024 closure rests on the A3+X24 route with C1a as corroborating harness, or a future bucketed long run; (4) h = 5 measured INFEASIBLE (~2.2yr) — the descent (C1b) must carry h >= 5. CONDITIONAL on: stand-in prime (see official_row_primes_pinning).
nodes.attack_surface: the F2/E37 MITM machinery at n = 1024 with disk-backed hashing if needed; SOLO
nodes.falsifier: a real trade found at an official prime (then absorbency counts it — budget ~1e5 per h)

nodes.title: C1b THE CORE: the descent-injection certificate (verified seed identity)
nodes.statement: VERIFIED SEED (sympy-exact): under the squaring pushforward A |-> A_2 (A_2(X^2) = (-1)^h A(X)A(-X)), a trade B = A + c maps to a BAND PAIR: B_2 - A_2 = ±(c(A(X)+A(-X)) + c^2) — Y-degree exactly floor(h/2). So level-n trades descend to level-n/2 band pairs (budget h/2, saturating in ~log h levels) — but ONLY log2(1024/64) = 4 LEVELS are needed to reach the exhaustively-censusable domain n = 64 (pilot-verified feasible). THE CERTIFICATE ALGORITHM: (i) enumerate band pairs at the bottom (n = 64, budget delta_4) — census machinery extended; (ii) for each bottom candidate, check lift-compatibility up the 4 levels (each root has 2 square-root preimages; the lift must itself satisfy the trade/band condition at each level — a per-candidate bounded check); (iii) level-n trades inject into (bottom pairs) x (compatible lift data) — THE INJECTION LEMMA is the one new statement (my pushforward identity is its seed; the antipodal-paired roots branch to the toral/paid side per X24's mechanism and must be tracked — C1c). CALIBRATION GROUND TRUTH: the pilot's exact n = 32 eliminant and the n = 16/32/64 censuses. CORRECTION (roadmap-lane, delta-recursion analysis): the band budget saturates — delta_{k+1} = floor((h + delta_k)/2) hits the vacuous h-1 after ~log2(h) levels, retaining only h/2^k coefficients of information. The '4 levels to n = 64' claim was WRONG. True design: k* = min(log2 h - 1, levels available); the descent is informative and bottom-censusable for h <= ~8-10 (e.g. h = 4: one level, bottom n = 512 with a 2-coefficient condition, MITM-countable; h = 8: two levels, bottom n = 256, borderline). LARGE h (>= ~16) IS NOT COVERED by this route — nor by C1a's direct MITM. If the window audit returns H_max > ~10, a third piece is required: the large-h emptiness lemma (heuristic margin: hundreds of bits; the obstruction count h-1 grows with h). The injection-lemma brief is HELD until the audit lands — its H_max decides the brief's shape. PROVED (Fable agent, D1-D8 chain, 19/19 replayed): exact band budget delta_k = h - ceil(h/2^k); exhaustive case split (the diagonal branch = Q = -P, paid); exact lift characterization with THE TERMINAL-LIFT COLLAPSE — <= 2 lifts per bottom pair (h even), <= 4 (h odd), replacing the naive 2^{2h}, tight in the toys; the injection has a linear-time inverse; soundness theorem with exact k*(h) and bottom parameters; collision exclusion whenever 2^j | h. Validated end-to-end: 32->16 and 64->32 (h=4) recover the pilot's exact toral counts (28/120, non-toral empty); 16->8 (h=3, F17) recovers all 352 trades including 16 paid diagonals. HONEST CORRECTION (mine, again): the bottom census at n = 1024 stops at h ~ 6 (2^38 work), not ~8-10 — C1b certifies h = 5, 6. Gaps G-C2/C3/C4 named in the note.

nodes.title: THE WIDENED GAP: certification for h in (~10, A], A <= 261
nodes.statement: With H_max = A (audit verdict), the certification pipelines cover: C1a direct MITM h = 4(-5); C1b descent h <= ~8-10 (band saturation). The range h in (~10, A] (A = 67/133/261 per rate) is covered by NEITHER — and the crude route (value_set_partner_cap x C(n,h) cores) reduces back to the terminal bound. ROUTES: (a) a large-h emptiness/exclusion THEOREM (first moment empty by thousands of bits; h-1 obstruction conditions overwhelm the C(n,h) pattern entropy above h ~ some explicit H0 — find the worst-case tool); (b) a scalable certificate valid at all h (the descent is not); (c) consumer-side: resolve the h <= A vs 2h <= A micro-lemma and check whether large-h trades enter R_PTE at full weight; (d) per-h budget arithmetic: 261 x ~1e5 << n^3 fits, so per-h bounds suffice — no aggregation cleverness needed. GAP PINNED (micro-lemma 5/5): h <= A reading PROVED (2h <= A wrong) — the certification gap is the full (10, A] with A = 67/133/261; its (100, A] tail additionally lies outside the frozen W3 grammar window at rates 1/4 and 1/8 (nonempty both), empty at 1/16. FRONTIER UPDATE (C1b proved + route-a autopsy): the gap is now h in (6, A] — C1b certifies h = 5, 6 (bottom census 2^38 at n=1024), C1a h = 4. Route (a) is DEAD (m1_trade_bijection: a bijection, not a bound). Live assets: the ~3.3e3-active-cores-per-h budget target (quantified), the M2 lever at extension rows, the first-moment margin (thousands of bits). This node is now the single largest open item on the (A) side.

nodes.title: PROVED (micro-lemma, 5/5): h <= A is the correct reading; 2h <= A is WRONG
nodes.statement: With agreement sets S_f, S_g (|.| = A = k+t), overlap r: the canonical trade is P = S_f \ S_g, Q = S_g \ S_f, so h = A - r; distinct degree-<k codewords agree in <= k-1 points => r <= k-1 => t < h <= A. The trade SUPPORT 2h = 2(A-r) splits across TWO agreement sets and runs up to 2A. Verified via explicit F_101 constructions realizing both extremes (r = 0: h = A; r = k-1: h = t+1). CONSEQUENCE: H_max = A = 67/133/261 confirmed; the beyond-grammar range (100, A] is nonempty at rates 1/4 AND 1/8 (261, 133), empty at 1/16 (67 < 100).

nodes.statement: For the averaged c=2 quotient-box family, the shared box charge amortizes by at least 429,645,547 bits relative to the single-word floor at sigma*.

Gain needs a heavy-fiber theorem (forbidden, circular). See aqb_coupled_family_entropy_manifest_payload/refutation.md.

nodes.title: e1 N'=256: density bound (WEAKENED from the zero-survivor cert)
nodes.statement: WEAKENED (consumer-tolerance, verified): e1_fullness needs only that non-quotient e1 collisions are o(1)-SPARSE relative to the value set (density), NOT a zero-survivor folded cert. The N'=256 dim-128 {-2..2}-box ZERO-cert is (a) likely IMPOSSIBLE at |F|<2^256 (random model: ~2^48 expected non-quotient box collisions; BKZ inconclusive) and (b) UNNECESSARY: 2^48 / value-set(~2^249) = 2^-201 = o(1), so e1_fullness holds regardless. ACHIEVABLE ROUTE (from e1_fullness's own statement): the density/typicality bound — norm_threshold_ext, split-prime transfer, or a bad-prime union bound — over the admissible prime family. The dim-128 exact lattice enumeration is over-engineered; do not pursue it. (N'=128 zero-cert genuinely HELD: lambda_1=31.67>16.)

nodes.notes: OPEN + HARD (Modal fpylll): BKZ shortest = 18.8 < radius 22.6 but inf-norm 4 (NOT a box vector) -> plain lambda_1 INCONCLUSIVE (cone-restricted/box bound needed, as reframing flagged). Count heuristic 2^48 box collisions NOT confirmed (BKZ found none). Needs a bounded-coefficient box enumeration or a cone-restricted dual cert to resolve certify-vs-collision. | RE-POSED (over-strong, verified): the zero-cert is impossible AND unnecessary; e1_fullness needs o(1)-sparsity (2^-201), delivered by the DENSITY route (norm-threshold / split-prime / bad-prime union). Deprioritize the dim-128 enumeration. | PROVED (Pro W3, verified): bad-prime union bound gives E S_p <= 2^64.2 at N'=256 => o(1)-sparse for all but p^-0.24 of admissible primes. Density/typicality route.

nodes.title: ef_fullfield_bridge (the s6 F1 typecheck)
nodes.statement: Every reduced horizontal component of the extension-fiber incidence variety X with TRIVIAL G-stabilizer, satisfying noncontainment, and not confined to a proper intermediate field, is a genuinely full-field extension fiber to which ext_pole_floor applies. Equivalently (s6_extension_lift.md F1): there is no genuinely-F obstruction of non-pole, non-confined type — the honest typecheck seam where a new ledger column (S9) would otherwise appear. nodes.title: Petal residue-kernel linear bound
nodes.statement: In the L1 coset-chart full-petal residue bridge, the residue-line kernel K_{I,d}=ker(pi_{>d} R_{I,d}) satisfies the proved Lemma 13 bound dim K <= c+1, where c=d-ell.

nodes.notes: AUDIT FLAG RESOLVED (verified): Lemma 13 (dim K <= c+1, i.e. rank floor r >= ell) holds PERFECTLY in-regime — verified ell=3,t=5, c=2..8: r>=ell and dim_K in {0,0,1,3,5,7,9} all <= c+1 (tight at c=8). The apparent census discrepancy was OUT OF SCOPE: the census's ell=2,t=3 has d=c+ell > (t-1)ell-1=3 for c>=2, collapsing the residue space to <ell columns so r<ell is forced by dimensions. Lemma 13 is SOUND; the l1 note (line 91) confirms the floor is never violated in-regime.

CLOSES given P5-CL: every cofactor family of dim > absolute B_pet is QUOTIENT/coset/signed/cyclotomic chargeable (sparse coeff support signature); then uncharged count is c-independent, total <= 2^M poly(M) q^{B_pet}=n^{O(1)}. SHARED THEME: needs quotient/coset chargeability.

Needs a REFINED census separating charged/uncharged + flatness of the uncharged count in c. | LEMMA-13 FLAG RESOLVED: the census raw dim_K growth is EXPECTED (Lemma 13 permits dim K up to c+1, and it's tight at large c: dim_K ~ c in-regime). Confirms the census must measure the UNCHARGED residual (after charging paid coset families), NOT raw dim_K. Refined census config: use ell>=3 (so c=2..8 is in Lemma 13's regime d<=(t-1)ell-1); classify charged vs uncharged; test uncharged flatness. | AMBERED: decomposed into its Pro-reduced obligation(s); see reqs.
