nodes.attack_surface: finite-union counting rule for classified squarefree kernel points
nodes.falsifier: a finite classification with each uncharged class uniformly polynomial but whose union grows beyond the same uniform-polynomial regime

nodes.title: Petal residue-kernel realizable sparsity
nodes.statement: Across the coset-chart corridor, the squarefree locator points inside the residue-line kernel that correspond to exact full-petal extras are bounded by a polynomial whose exponent is independent of the excess c. This is reduced to the proved classification-to-sparsity counting rule and the remaining squarefree-kernel classification payload.
nodes.attack_surface: assembly from classification-to-sparsity soundness and the remaining squarefree kernel classification payload
nodes.falsifier: a corridor family where squarefree realizable locator points inside the residue kernel grow beyond the uniform polynomial budget

nodes.title: Petal squarefree kernel classification payload
nodes.statement: Classify the squarefree locator points inside the residue-line kernels across the coset-chart corridor into charged families and uncharged families with polynomial bounds whose exponents are independent of the excess c. This is reduced to the proved classification-ledger soundness rule plus the remaining actual squarefree classification ledger payload.
nodes.attack_surface: assembly from the classification-ledger soundness rule and the actual structural classification of squarefree kernel points or proof-grade transfer from off-laptop certificates
nodes.falsifier: an unclassified or uncharged squarefree kernel family whose size has exponent growing with c

nodes.title: Petal residue-line uniformity
nodes.statement: In the coset-chart residue bridge for the growing-excess petal problem, the residue-line contribution has uniformly bounded realizable full-petal extras over the corridor range; the ambient kernel dimension is controlled only by the proved linear Lemma 13 bound dim K<=c+1, and literal kernel flatness is refuted in small coset rows.
nodes.attack_surface: assemble the proved linear kernel accounting with realizable-extra uniformity; do not use the refuted ambient-kernel flatness route
nodes.falsifier: a calibration row or theorem where exact realizable full-petal extras grow with c beyond the allowed polynomial budget

nodes.title: Petal squarefree classification-ledger soundness
nodes.statement: If a ledger covers every squarefree realizable locator point in the residue-line kernels by disjoint charged or uncharged class records, every charged record cites an already paid family, every uncharged record has a polynomial bound with exponent independent of c, and the number of uncharged records is independent of c, then petal_squarefree_kernel_classification_payload holds.
nodes.attack_surface: formal coverage and charged/uncharged dispatch for Petal squarefree classification ledgers
nodes.falsifier: a complete valid classification ledger while the squarefree-kernel classification payload fails

nodes.title: Petal realizable extras inject into residue kernel
nodes.statement: In the L1 coset-chart full-petal residue bridge, every exact realizable full-petal extra determines a squarefree locator point lying in the residue-line kernel, and this assignment is injective.
nodes.attack_surface: Lemma 8 full-petal rank certificate from the coset-chart bridge
nodes.falsifier: two distinct realizable full-petal extras with the same locator point, or a realizable extra whose locator does not lie in the residue-line kernel

nodes.title: SOV grid character-sum bound (affine-piece route REFUTED)
nodes.statement: RE-POSED (Pro P2+P3, verified): affine-piece route REFUTED (q>n incidence); grid-sum FALSE for the canonical trace char (F_{17^32}: 496/512 trace-zero points, cell density 0.506 at h=21, VERIFIED). single_obstruction survives (scan UNIFORM_CONSISTENT). NEW target P3': prove the grid character-sum bound after removing TRACE-FLAT/SUBFIELD-NORM gates (a!=0 with x->Tr(ax) constant on a positive-density cell) IN ADDITION to quotient/pullback/dihedral, then a Lane-1 power-sum / Lane-2 sheaf bound on the residual. SHARED THEME: trace/subfield-flat is a PAID gate (cf. dli P2).
nodes.attack_surface: actual anchored-core coordinate decomposition and exceptional-piece budget proof
nodes.falsifier: a forced-root conditioning cell with no such partition or with exceptional mass above the character-sum budget
nodes.notes: SOV SCAN DONE (Modal, evidence): UNIFORM_CONSISTENT across ALL (row,h) — n=128/256, h=21..40, 60k samples/config; collision ratio ~1.00 everywhere, max fiber 3-15, any_heavy_collisions=FALSE. Verdict UNIFORM_CONSISTENT_LEMMA_SHAPE_SUPPORTED — the value-distribution lemma shape is confirmed (small fibers). Evidence-only; the affine-piece partition proof (W-shaped char-sum) remains the close. | W2 (verified): affine-piece route REFUTED (q>n incidence obstruction); re-posed to the grid character-sum bound. Downstream single_obstruction NOT refuted. | AMBERED: decomposed into its Pro-reduced obligation(s); see reqs. | Shared gate subfield_trace_paid_gate PROVED (Pro T1); this node now reduces to its prime-field/non-degenerate residual only.

nodes.title: SOV active-core obstruction vanishing
nodes.statement: Every active h-core in the intended h>20 band corresponds to a finite h-trade whose locator L satisfies the forced-root obstruction equations, so all obstruction coordinates from sov_forced_root_recursion_algebra vanish.
nodes.attack_surface: connect active-core equations to the finite h-trade square-shift model used by X79, uniformly for h in the intended band
nodes.falsifier: an active h-core whose locator is not in the square-shift trade model or has a nonzero forced-root obstruction coordinate

nodes.title: SOV first-obstruction coefficient sensitivity
nodes.statement: For the forced-root obstruction construction, changing only the coefficient of X^{h-1} in the locator L leaves the forced root unchanged and changes the first obstruction O_{h-1} by exactly the negative of that coefficient change. Hence O_{h-1} is the correct coordinate-sensitive obstruction to use for the value-set problem.
nodes.attack_surface: triangular forced-root recursion and coefficient inspection
nodes.falsifier: a unit change in the X^{h-1} coefficient that changes the forced root or fails to change O_{h-1} by -1

nodes.title: SOV first-obstruction value-set equidistribution
nodes.statement: For official-shape rows and h in (20,A], the first obstruction map O_{h-1} on anchored cores has sufficiently small fibers. Equivalently, the value set of O_{h-1} is large enough to prevent a super-budget concentration of active cores in any one value fiber.
nodes.attack_surface: assembly from first-obstruction sensitivity and the remaining value-distribution/fiber theorem
nodes.falsifier: a calibration or official-shape row where O_{h-1} has a fiber above the active-core budget, or a structural family of anchored cores on which O_{h-1} is constant

nodes.title: SOV forced-root recursion algebra
nodes.statement: For every h over odd characteristic, the monic degree-h square-root recursion uniquely recovers the degree-h polynomial S forced by the top h+1 coefficients of a monic degree-2h locator L, and the obstruction vector is exactly the list of coefficients of S^2-L in degrees 1 through h-1. In the X79 shifted-constant trade model the recursion returns the midpoint and all obstructions vanish.
nodes.attack_surface: symbolic triangular coefficient recursion; local verifier replays the generalized X79 gate for several h
nodes.falsifier: a degree 2h locator whose top coefficients do not determine the forced root by the printed recursion, or a shifted-constant X79 trade with a nonzero obstruction

nodes.title: SOV h-1 fiber Fourier duality
nodes.statement: For any forced-root conditioned anchored-core fiber and the map c(L)=[X^{h-1}]L to the row field, the largest fiber of c is bounded by the uniform term plus the average of the nontrivial additive character sums of c.
nodes.attack_surface: finite-field Fourier inversion on the coefficient map
nodes.falsifier: a finite map to a field whose fiber size exceeds the Fourier-inversion bound

nodes.title: SOV fiber bound to active-core budget
nodes.statement: The quantitative fiber bound supplied by sov_obstruction_equidistribution is below the per-h active-core budget for every h in (20,A] at the official-shape rows.
nodes.attack_surface: exact arithmetic once the value-set bound has constants
nodes.falsifier: a row or h value where the proved fiber bound is still too large for the active-core budget used by midlarge_h20_A

nodes.title: SOV nonconstant affine character cancellation
nodes.statement: For any finite-dimensional affine space over the row field, any nontrivial additive character, and any affine-linear coefficient map with nonzero linear part, the nontrivial character sum over the affine space is zero. For a disjoint partition into such affine pieces plus an exceptional set, the total character sum is bounded by the exceptional-set size.
nodes.attack_surface: finite-field additive-character orthogonality on a nonconstant affine map
nodes.falsifier: a nonconstant affine-linear map over a finite field whose nontrivial additive character sum is nonzero

nodes.title: SOV first-obstruction value distribution
nodes.statement: For official-shape rows and h in (20,A], the coefficient-sensitive first obstruction O_{h-1} has fibers small enough on anchored cores to meet the active-core budget.
nodes.attack_surface: assembly from first-obstruction coefficient sensitivity and the remaining h-1 locator-coefficient distribution theorem
nodes.falsifier: a calibration or official-shape row where a fiber of O_{h-1} is above budget, or a structural constant-family for O_{h-1}

nodes.title: SOV h-1 affine-piece decomposition
nodes.statement: For every official-shape row, h in (20,A], and forced-root higher-coefficient conditioning cell of anchored-core locators, partition the cell into affine pieces on which c(L)=[X^{h-1}]L is nonconstant affine-linear, plus exceptional paid/norm-structured pieces whose total size is below the character-sum budget. This is reduced to affine-partition certificate soundness plus the remaining anchored-core partition payload.
nodes.attack_surface: assembly from proved affine-partition certificate soundness and the remaining anchored-core affine/paid-piece partition payload
nodes.falsifier: a conditioning cell with a large non-paid component on which the h-1 coefficient is constant or has no cancellative affine direction, or a defect in the partition certificate soundness rule

nodes.title: SOV forced-root obstruction correctness
nodes.statement: The generalized forced square-root recursion correctly produces the obstruction vector for every h in the intended band, and every active h-core forces the relevant obstruction coordinates to vanish.
nodes.attack_surface: assembly from the proved forced-root recursion algebra and the remaining active-core obstruction-vanishing theorem
nodes.falsifier: an active core in the intended h band whose forced-root obstruction vector does not vanish, or a mismatch with the banked h=5/X79 construction

nodes.title: SOV h-1 locator-coefficient distribution
nodes.statement: For official-shape rows and h in (20,A], the coefficient [X^{h-1}]L of anchored-core locators has fibers small enough, after conditioning on the forced-root higher coefficients, to meet the active-core budget once transported by first-obstruction sensitivity. This follows from finite Fourier fiber duality plus the anchored-core nontrivial character-sum bound for this coefficient.
nodes.attack_surface: assembly from sov_hminus1_fiber_fourier_duality and the remaining anchored-core character-sum bound
nodes.falsifier: a calibration or official-shape row where the h-1 coefficient has a fiber above budget after the forced-root conditioning

nodes.title: SOV affine-piece partition certificate soundness
nodes.statement: If a certificate partitions every forced-root conditioning cell into disjoint affine pieces where c(L)=[X^{h-1}]L has nonzero linear part, plus paid/norm-structured exceptional pieces whose total size is below the declared character-sum budget, then sov_hminus1_affine_piece_decomposition holds.
nodes.attack_surface: finite partition and affine-linear certificate semantics for SOV conditioned cells
nodes.falsifier: a verified affine/exceptional partition certificate that fails to imply the SOV h-1 affine-piece decomposition

nodes.title: SOV h-1 anchored-core character-sum bound
nodes.statement: For every official-shape row, h in (20,A], and forced-root higher-coefficient conditioning cell, the nontrivial additive character sums of c(L)=[X^{h-1}]L over anchored cores are small enough that the Fourier-duality fiber bound is below the active-core budget. This is reduced to a proved affine-piece cancellation lemma plus the remaining anchored-core affine/paid-piece decomposition.
nodes.attack_surface: assembly from affine-piece cancellation and the remaining decomposition/classification of conditioned anchored-core cells
nodes.falsifier: a forced-root conditioning cell with nontrivial character sums large enough to create an above-budget h-1 coefficient fiber

nodes.title: F-valued word source => e-interleaved base-list witness (Pro, verified)
nodes.statement: Every trivial-stabilizer noncontainment non-tower horizontal component with F-valued word source is, at its generic point, an e=[E:B]-interleaved base-field multiplication-slice witness (syndrome commutes with B-basis expansion since D subset B; mult-by-z = M_z; noncontainment preserved). The ordinary-base-list version is FALSE (proved extension-line counterexample).

nodes.title: ext_pole_floor covers e-interleaved base-list witnesses (the S9 seam)
nodes.statement: REFINED (Pro round 3, verified): the interleaved list-counting is EXACT — the e-interleaved witness has effective size L_eff = #{(A_1..A_e): |cap A_i| >= a} = the ordinary EXTENSION-CODE list size |Lambda(C_E, 1-a/n, f+zg)| (L2 bridge), NOT L^e; interleaving filters the product by common support. The pole floor then applies with N_int(L_eff) = ceil(L_eff X /(X + kappa_int L_eff)). SOLE remaining seam: ExtPoleFloorAppliesToWeilRestrictedInterleaving — the Paper D floor PROOF is functorial under Weil restriction (accepts a B-defined Weil-restricted interleaved witness as a base list). FOR THE GEOMETRIC EXCLUSION (ef_pole_free_cycle_exclusion) the SINGLETON suffices: L_eff=1 => N_int(1)=ceil(X/(X+kappa))=1 (VERIFIED, all X>0) => component meets D_E. So ef exclusion closes modulo the singleton functoriality; only the numerator LEDGER needs the full L_eff gate.
nodes.falsifier: an e-interleaved base-list witness escaping ext_pole_floor -- the S9 class.
nodes.notes: Pro follow-up brief issued (same ef thread): does N(L) apply to the interleaved witness effective list size? Prove (A) or exhibit S9 (B). The crux is pure list-counting of the multiplication-slice vs N(L). Closes ef->f1. | PRO ROUND 3 (verified): list-counting EXACT (L_eff = extension-code list, not L^e; interleaving is diagonal not Cartesian). Singleton exclusion N_int(1)=1 VERIFIED. Reduced to Weil-restriction functoriality of the Paper D floor proof (singleton for exclusion) — a scope property of an EXISTING proof, checkable by inspecting it. | PROVED (Pro W2, verified): the Paper D simple-pole floor is Weil-functorial for the singleton multiplication-slice witness (CA-bad construction verified, numerator L=1); no S9 class. Closes ef->f1.

nodes.title: DLI odd-evaluation discrepancy
nodes.statement: For every central profile and every nonzero frequency, the sequence of low-degree odd evaluations P_lambda(sigma(y)) on the square-root section has enough discrepancy control away from Dirichlet peaks to support the DLI log-integral geometric-mean bound with total loss sum_j eps_j=o(t).

nodes.falsifier: a central profile and nonzero frequency whose odd-evaluation sequence remains near Dirichlet peaks on a positive-density set of section coordinates, forcing a linear-in-t loss

nodes.title: DLI actual odd-phase nontriviality payload
nodes.statement: For every central profile, nonzero frequency, DLI harmonic, and relevant square-root component, the actual odd-evaluation phase P_lambda(sigma(y)) is not of Artin-Schreier form g^p-g+c. This is reduced to the proved polar-obstruction soundness rule plus the remaining actual DLI polar-obstruction certificate payload.

nodes.falsifier: an actual central-profile component where P_lambda(sigma(y)) equals g^p-g+c

nodes.title: DLI actual reduced-phase manifest payload
nodes.statement: Provide the actual DLI reduced-phase manifest: for every central profile, nonzero frequency, DLI harmonic, and relevant square-root component, the Artin-Schreier-reduced local expansion of P_lambda(sigma(y)), a positive pole order prime to p, a certified polar-divisor majorant for the same tuple, and an o(t) harmonic total for the majorants.
nodes.attack_surface: actual project-specific local expansions, Artin-Schreier reduction, pole witnesses, majorant rows, and harmonic summation
nodes.falsifier: a missing tuple, no prime-to-p reduced pole, a majorant below the true reduced polar divisor, or a harmonic total not o(t)
nodes.notes: Pro brief issued (deep analytic): the Deligne-Weil reduced-phase manifest — per-tuple prime-to-p reduced pole + conductor majorant + o(t) harmonic sum, giving geometric-mean equidistribution at -2log2. Calibrated flat. Closes dli->ejm->pcf->b2b primitive core. | Pro (verified): pole+conductor parts PROVED (dli_phase_pole_conductor_manifest); the o(t) harmonic total is the REAL gap (dli_harmonic_conductor_ledger) — ordinary Deligne non-saving at section scale (sqrt q/N=2^87). Target C. | NOTE: the harmonic-total obligation WEAKENED to polynomial-loss per level (o(t) over 34 levels); the sharp geometric-mean route is over-engineered.

nodes.title: DLI circle log-integral constant
nodes.statement: The exact circle constant in DLI is int_0^1 log |cos(2 pi x)|^2 dx = -2 log 2.
nodes.attack_surface: classical one-dimensional integral
nodes.falsifier: a corrected evaluation of the circle integral

nodes.title: DLI actual odd-phase polar-obstruction payload
nodes.statement: For every central profile, nonzero frequency, DLI harmonic, and relevant square-root component, construct a local certificate showing that the Artin-Schreier-reduced odd-evaluation phase P_lambda(sigma(y)) has a positive pole order not divisible by p. This is reduced to a proved reduced-phase manifest soundness rule plus the remaining actual reduced-phase manifest.
nodes.attack_surface: assembly from reduced-phase manifest soundness and the remaining project-specific local expansion/cancellation manifest
nodes.falsifier: an actual DLI odd phase whose Artin-Schreier-reduced local expansions have no pole order prime to p

nodes.title: DLI odd-evaluation peak-mass discrepancy
nodes.statement: For every central profile and every nonzero frequency, the odd low-degree evaluations on the square-root section satisfy the truncated-log discrepancy and peak-mass tail bounds required by dli_truncated_log_transfer, with total error sum_j eps_j=o(t).

nodes.falsifier: a central profile and nonzero frequency for which odd evaluations put too much mass near Dirichlet peaks or violate the required truncated-log discrepancy bound

nodes.title: DLI odd-phase polar-obstruction soundness
nodes.statement: If, for each actual DLI odd-evaluation phase on each relevant square-root component, a certificate identifies a pole of the Artin-Schreier-reduced phase with positive order not divisible by p, then the phase is not of Artin-Schreier form g^p-g+c.

nodes.falsifier: a phase of Artin-Schreier form g^p-g+c whose reduced representative has a pole order prime to p

nodes.title: DLI odd-evaluation exponential-sum bound
nodes.statement: For every central profile and every nonzero frequency, the normalized odd low-degree evaluations on the square-root section have Weyl sums small enough, uniformly over the harmonic ranges required by dli_et_peak_mass_reduction, with total DLI error sum_j eps_j=o(t).

nodes.falsifier: a central profile, nonzero frequency, and harmonic range whose Weyl sum is too large to feed the peak-mass reduction

nodes.title: DLI actual odd-phase reduced-pole budget
nodes.statement: For every central profile, nonzero frequency, and DLI harmonic, the actual odd-evaluation phase on each square-root component is not of Artin-Schreier-trivial form, and its reduced polar divisor has harmonic total o(t). This is reduced to the proved harmonic ledger soundness rule plus the remaining actual nontriviality and polar-divisor ledger payloads.
nodes.attack_surface: assembly from DLI odd-phase nontriviality and reduced-pole harmonic ledger payloads
nodes.falsifier: an actual central-profile component where the phase is g^p-g+constant, or where the reduced polar-divisor harmonic total exceeds the DLI budget

nodes.title: DLI Deligne-to-Weyl transfer
nodes.statement: If, for every central profile, nonzero frequency, and harmonic m in the DLI ranges, the odd-evaluation phase on each square-root component defines a geometrically nontrivial bounded-conductor trace sheaf whose conductor budget has harmonic total o(t), then Deligne/Weil square-root cancellation gives the finite-frequency Weyl-sum bounds required by dli_odd_eval_exponential_sum_bound.
nodes.attack_surface: standard curve/sheaf exponential-sum transfer plus harmonic summation of the conductor budget
nodes.falsifier: a geometrically nontrivial bounded-conductor odd-evaluation phase satisfying the stated conductor budget but failing square-root Weyl cancellation

nodes.title: DLI truncated-log discrepancy transfer
nodes.statement: If the odd-evaluation sequence satisfies the stated truncated-log discrepancy bounds for the Dirichlet kernel and the stated peak-mass tail bounds near its singular peaks, with total error sum_j eps_j=o(t), then the DLI odd-evaluation discrepancy predicate follows.
nodes.attack_surface: deterministic truncation/layer-cake transfer from peak discrepancy to the logarithmic geometric-mean bound
nodes.falsifier: a sequence satisfying the truncated-log and peak-mass hypotheses but failing the corresponding log-integral lower bound

nodes.title: DLI reduced-phase manifest soundness
nodes.statement: If a reduced-phase manifest covers every central profile, nonzero frequency, DLI harmonic, and square-root component, gives an Artin-Schreier-reduced local pole of positive order prime to p for each tuple, gives a certified polar-divisor majorant for the same tuple, and proves the harmonic majorant total is o(t), then both dli_odd_phase_polar_obstruction_payload and dli_reduced_pole_majorant_table_payload hold.
nodes.attack_surface: schema soundness tying the DLI local polar obstruction and reduced-pole majorant table to one manifest
nodes.falsifier: a verified reduced-phase manifest satisfying the pole and majorant predicates while one DLI payload fails

nodes.title: DLI odd-phase noncollapse and conductor budget
nodes.statement: For every central profile, nonzero frequency, and harmonic m in the DLI ranges, the actual odd-evaluation phase P_lambda(sigma(y)) on the square-root section is geometrically nonconstant/non-Artin-Schreier on every relevant component, with conductor bounds whose harmonic total is o(t). This is reduced to the standard Artin-Schreier/conductor criterion plus the remaining reduced-pole and noncollapse budget for the actual phases.
nodes.attack_surface: assembly from dli_artin_schreier_conductor_criterion and dli_odd_phase_reduced_pole_budget
nodes.falsifier: a central profile and nonzero frequency where the odd-evaluation phase is geometrically trivial on a component, or where the conductor budget is too large for the DLI harmonic summation

nodes.title: DLI actual reduced-pole majorant table payload
nodes.statement: Construct the actual DLI reduced-pole majorant table: for every central profile, nonzero frequency, DLI harmonic, and square-root component, bound the Artin-Schreier-reduced polar divisor of P_lambda(sigma(y)) and prove that the harmonic sum of the table is o(t). This is reduced to a proved reduced-phase manifest soundness rule plus the remaining actual reduced-phase manifest.
nodes.attack_surface: assembly from reduced-phase manifest soundness and the remaining project-specific reduced local pole accounting manifest
nodes.falsifier: an actual DLI tuple whose reduced polar divisor exceeds the table entry, or a table whose harmonic total is not o(t)

nodes.title: DLI reduced-pole majorant-table soundness
nodes.statement: If a table covers every central profile, nonzero frequency, DLI harmonic, and square-root component; gives a certified upper bound for the Artin-Schreier-reduced polar divisor of each covered phase; and proves that the sum of those majorants over the DLI harmonic ranges is o(t), then dli_reduced_pole_harmonic_ledger holds.
nodes.attack_surface: formal coverage, domination, and summation rule for DLI reduced-pole majorant tables
nodes.falsifier: a complete certified majorant table with total o(t) while the actual reduced-pole harmonic ledger fails

nodes.title: DLI Erdos-Turan peak-mass reduction
nodes.statement: If the normalized odd-evaluation sequence satisfies the required finite-frequency Weyl-sum bounds up to the reciprocal scales used by the DLI peak neighborhoods, then Erdos-Turan plus dyadic annuli around the Dirichlet peaks gives the truncated-log discrepancy and peak-mass tail bounds required by dli_truncated_log_transfer.
nodes.attack_surface: deterministic interval-discrepancy and dyadic layer-cake reduction
nodes.falsifier: a sequence satisfying the stated Weyl-sum bounds but violating the required peak-mass or truncated-log discrepancy bound

nodes.title: DLI reduced-pole harmonic ledger
nodes.statement: For the same actual odd-evaluation phases, provide reduced Artin-Schreier polar-divisor bounds whose sum over the DLI harmonic ranges and components is o(t). This is reduced to a proved majorant-table soundness rule plus the remaining actual DLI reduced-pole majorant table payload.
nodes.attack_surface: assembly from the majorant-table soundness rule and actual reduced polar-divisor accounting for the DLI odd phases
nodes.falsifier: a harmonic range or component where the reduced polar-divisor contribution makes the total non-negligible

nodes.title: DLI Artin-Schreier conductor criterion
nodes.statement: For an additive phase f on a smooth curve component, the associated Artin-Schreier trace sheaf is geometrically trivial exactly when f differs from a constant by g^p-g; otherwise its conductor is bounded by the reduced polar divisor of f after Artin-Schreier reduction.
nodes.attack_surface: standard Artin-Schreier sheaf triviality and conductor bookkeeping
nodes.falsifier: a non-Artin-Schreier rational phase whose additive trace sheaf is geometrically trivial, or whose conductor exceeds its reduced polar-divisor bound

nodes.title: DLI odd-phase budget ledger soundness
nodes.statement: If a DLI odd-phase ledger covers every central profile, nonzero frequency, harmonic, and square-root component; marks every phase non-Artin-Schreier; and supplies reduced polar-divisor bounds whose harmonic total is o(t), then dli_odd_phase_reduced_pole_budget holds.
nodes.attack_surface: formal coverage and summation rule for DLI reduced-pole ledgers
nodes.falsifier: a complete ledger with all phases marked nontrivial and total o(t) while the reduced-pole budget statement fails

nodes.title: DLI log-integral equidistribution
nodes.statement: For every central profile and every nonzero frequency, the odd low-degree evaluations on the square-root section satisfy the near-circle log-integral geometric-mean bound -sum_y log |mu_hat_y(P_lambda(sigma(y)))|^2 >= 2(L_j-eps_j) log q - O(1), with sum_j eps_j=o(t).

nodes.falsifier: a profile/frequency pair whose Dirichlet factors stay near peaks often enough that the total loss is linear in t

nodes.title: DLI phase-side pole+conductor manifest (Pro, verified)
nodes.statement: For each DLI tuple: the Artin-Schreier reduced phase hP_lambda has a prime-to-p pole of order ed (d odd <p, e a 2-power sqrt pullback), surviving nondegeneracy, with conductor majorant ed + C_tame. Parts 1-3 of the reduced-phase manifest.

nodes.title: DLI polynomial-loss per-level bound (WEAKENED from sharp geometric-mean)
nodes.statement: SCOPED (Pro P2, verified): fixed-freq 2nd-moment flatness S_{j,lambda}=o(L_j) is FALSE over extension fields (F_{p^2}: a trace-zero lambda + subfield-valued section collapses psi to 1, S=Omega(L_j), VERIFIED) but HOLDS prime-field-wise (Tr=id, no collapse) — consistent with b2b's 'fixed prime q'. Prime rows: still needs the fixed-frequency Bohr bound (P2-A). EXTENSION rows (KoalaBear sextic F_{p^6}) need Weil-restriction to base F_p OR a proved no-subfield-trace-kernel-collapse hypothesis. SHARED THEME: the trace/subfield-flat frequencies are a PAID gate (cf. sov P3).
